project_goal.value: RESTful API service enforcing fine-grained RBAC/ABAC authorization for enterprise SaaS tenants with real-time policy evaluation and SOC 2/ISO 27001 audit logging
project_goal.rationale: Core product differentiation in multi-tenant SaaS authorization

target_users.value: SaaS application backend services and their tenant admins via management API
target_users.rationale: Primary consumers are machine clients and admin operators

project_class.rationale: Pure API service with no frontend; authorization decisions exposed over REST

capabilities.rationale: Decision engine, policy storage, JWT auth, public API surface, and audit analytics

complexity_level.rationale: ABAC evaluation with tenant isolation and sub-5ms SLA at scale

risk_level.rationale: Authorization failures can expose cross-tenant data; SOC 2 and ISO 27001 in scope

data_sensitivity.rationale: Policy rules and audit events contain sensitive access control data

external_exposure.rationale: All endpoints require signed JWT; no anonymous access

access_model.value: JWT-based service auth with per-tenant JWKS rotation; admin endpoints require additional HMAC-signed audit proof
access_model.rationale: Tenant isolation requires per-tenant key material

feature_scope.value: Policy rule CRUD, real-time allow/deny evaluation endpoint, RBAC role management, ABAC attribute binding, audit event stream, compliance report generation
feature_scope.rationale: Full authorization lifecycle management

mvp_scope.value: Policy evaluation endpoint with RBAC, JWT auth, per-tenant isolation, audit log persistence, and basic compliance report export
mvp_scope.rationale: Minimum viable for first SaaS tenant onboarding

security_baseline.value: TLS 1.3, AES-256 encryption at rest for policy_rules and audit_events, JWKS rotation every 24h, row-level security per tenant_context
security_baseline.rationale: SOC 2 CC6 and ISO 27001 A.10 cryptographic requirements

frontend_stack.value: none
frontend_stack.rationale: API service only; no frontend component

backend_stack.rationale: Low-latency evaluation loops and efficient goroutine concurrency for parallel policy checks

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 for policy store with row-level security; Redis 7 for evaluation cache and decision memoization
data_platform.rationale: Relational policy storage with caching for sub-5ms SLA

hosting_target.value: AWS ECS Fargate with Application Load Balancer
hosting_target.rationale: Managed container runtime with auto-scaling for bursty authorization workloads

privacy_retention_policy.value: Audit events retained 2 years hot in PostgreSQL, then archived to S3 Glacier with 7-year retention per SOC 2 evidence requirements
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: SOC 2 audit evidence and ISO 27001 log retention mandates

future_scope.value: GraphQL evaluation API, Rego policy language support, OPAL-based real-time policy sync
future_scope.rationale: Roadmap items for year 2 enterprise expansion

constraints.value: P99 evaluation latency <= 5ms at 10K RPS; all data must stay in us-east-1 for GDPR data-residency; no external policy engine calls on the hot path
constraints.rationale: SLA and data-residency requirements from first enterprise customer

observability_baseline.value: Datadog APM with trace-level policy evaluation spans; custom metrics for decision latency, policy cache hit rate, and per-tenant request volume
observability_baseline.rationale: Corporate monitoring standard and SLA tracking

execution_preference.value: Two-week sprints with blue-green deployments gated on latency regression tests
execution_preference.rationale: Zero-downtime required for always-on authorization service

compliance_context.value: SOC 2 Type II (CC6, CC7, CC9), ISO 27001 A.9/A.10/A.12, GDPR Article 32 data-residency and processing security
compliance_context.rationale: Enterprise customers require SOC 2 and ISO 27001 attestations

project.goal: Sub-5ms RBAC/ABAC authorization API with per-tenant isolation and immutable audit trail
project.target_users: SaaS backend services and tenant administrators

backend.framework: Gin with custom policy evaluation middleware
backend.api: RESTful with OpenAPI 3.0 and versioned routes

security.auth: Custom JWT with per-tenant JWKS rotation
security.encryption: AES-256 at rest, TLS 1.3 in transit

reasoner_reviews.product: The authorization service has a clear mandate: serve allow/deny decisions with sub-5ms latency and produce immutable audit trails. The compliance report feature is critical for SOC 2 auditor access but should not share the hot evaluation path.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: In-process ABAC evaluation in Go avoids network hops that would break the 5ms SLA. Redis memoization of policy decisions per (principal, resource, action) triple is the right cache key. PostgreSQL RLS enforcing tenant_context boundaries is non-negotiable for the multi-tenant isolation guarantee.
reasoner_reviews.security: Per-tenant JWKS rotation every 24 hours reduces blast radius if a tenant key is compromised. The audit_events table must be append-only with a PostgreSQL row-level trigger preventing UPDATE or DELETE to satisfy SOC 2 CC7.2. GDPR data residency must be enforced at the ALB layer by restricting traffic to us-east-1 endpoints.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: The 5ms P99 target at 10K RPS is aggressive. Pre-compiled policy evaluation graphs cached in Redis and warm Fargate tasks with connection pooling via PgBouncer are necessary. Load tests must gate every deploy.
reasoner_reviews.critic: Round 2 addressed the missing ABAC attribute-binding endpoint but the compliance report generation path still blocks on the audit_events table under high write load. Recommend a read replica dedicated to batch compliance queries.

backend.service_design: Three Go packages: policy_engine (ABAC evaluation tree, role resolver), tenant_router (JWT validation, tenant_context injection), audit_writer (append-only event publisher). Gin middleware chain: rate-limit -> JWT validate -> tenant-inject -> policy-eval -> audit-emit.
backend.api_patterns: POST /v1/evaluate (decision), GET /v1/policies (list), POST /v1/policies (create), DELETE /v1/policies/:id, GET /v1/roles, POST /v1/role-assignments, GET /v1/audit-events (paginated), POST /v1/compliance-reports (async job)
backend.background_jobs: Nightly Prefect job aggregates audit_events into immutable compliance report snapshots; S3 upload with SHA-256 manifest for auditor download
backend.scaling_notes: ECS auto-scaling on p95 evaluation latency metric; PgBouncer connection pool of 50 per Fargate task; Redis cluster mode for decision cache sharding across 3 nodes

security.auth_design: Per-tenant JWKS endpoint; access tokens signed RS256 with 15-minute TTL; refresh tokens stored server-side with binding to IP subnet
security.authorization_model: Service-level RBAC for API consumers; ABAC within each tenant policy space; admin endpoints require additional HSM-signed proof-of-possession header
security.audit_and_logging_controls: audit_events table with PostgreSQL trigger blocking UPDATE/DELETE; CloudTrail for AWS-level actions; Datadog log correlation for cross-event audit trails
security.security_risks: Cross-tenant policy rule leakage if RLS policy has a logic gap; Redis cache poisoning if cache keys do not include tenant_context hash

data.entities: policy_rules (id, tenant_context_id FK, rule_type ENUM rbac/abac, subject_expr, resource_expr, action_expr, effect ENUM allow/deny, priority, created_at), role_assignments (id, tenant_context_id FK, principal_identity_id FK, role_name, granted_by, expires_at), permission_scopes (id, tenant_context_id FK, scope_name, actions TEXT[]), audit_events (id, tenant_context_id FK, principal_identity_id, resource_target, action, decision ENUM allow/deny, policy_matched_id, evaluated_at) APPEND ONLY, tenant_contexts (id, jwks_url, encryption_key_arn, tier, created_at)
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL 16 with RLS; audit_events in time-partitioned table (monthly) for efficient archival; Redis 7 for policy decision cache with 60s TTL
data.retention_and_deletion: audit_events: 2-year hot retention then S3 Glacier archive; policy_rules: soft-delete only, 90-day tombstone before permanent removal per GDPR erasure workflow

devops.deployment_topology: ECS Fargate cluster in us-east-1a/1c/1d behind ALB; PostgreSQL Aurora multi-AZ; Redis 7 ElastiCache cluster mode; S3 for compliance report archives
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: golangci-lint, go test -race, load test (k6 at 10K RPS), Docker build, ECR push, ECS rolling deploy with canary 10% traffic shift gated on p99 latency alarm
devops.observability_stack: Datadog APM traces on every evaluation request; custom metrics: eval_latency_ms histogram, policy_cache_hit_ratio, audit_write_lag_ms, active_tenant_count; PagerDuty alert on p99 > 8ms

ISS-001.title: Compliance report generation contends with audit write path on primary DB
ISS-001.detail: Compliance report queries run full scans over audit_events on the same PostgreSQL primary that handles real-time write traffic, causing 40ms spikes in evaluation latency during nightly batch runs

history.note: Identified during load test with concurrent report generation
Read replica added to plan but not yet in topology diagram

ISS-002.title: Redis cache key does not include tenant_context hash
ISS-002.detail: Current cache key is (principal_id, resource, action) without tenant scope. In a multi-tenant environment this could serve a cached allow decision from Tenant A to a Tenant B principal requesting the same resource path

history.note: Discovered during security review of cache eviction logic

focus_issues.title: Compliance report generation contends with audit write path on primary DB
focus_issues.detail: Compliance report queries run full scans over audit_events on same primary DB as real-time write path

focus_issues.title: Redis cache key does not include tenant_context hash
focus_issues.detail: Missing tenant scope in cache key creates potential cross-tenant decision bleed

latest_recommendations: Add dedicated Aurora read replica for compliance report queries to isolate from real-time write path
Prefix all Redis evaluation cache keys with SHA-256 of tenant_context_id to prevent cross-tenant cache collisions

convergence_state.reason: Two high-severity security and performance issues remain unresolved

previous_audits.summary: Strong policy evaluation architecture but two high-severity issues block passage: DB contention during compliance reports and missing tenant scope in Redis cache keys.
strengths: Go in-process ABAC evaluation correctly avoids network hops on hot path
PostgreSQL RLS with per-tenant_context enforcement is architecturally sound
Append-only audit_events trigger design satisfies SOC 2 CC7.2
concerns: Compliance report batch queries share the primary DB write path
Redis cache keys lack tenant isolation, creating potential security regression
blocking_issues: Cross-tenant cache collision risk in Redis decision store
DB latency spikes during nightly compliance report generation
recommendations: Provision Aurora read replica and route all compliance report SELECT queries to replica endpoint
Change Redis key format to sha256(tenant_context_id):principal_id:resource:action

previous_plan.title: Enterprise Authorization Policy Service - Round 2
previous_plan.executive_summary: Go-based RBAC/ABAC evaluation API on ECS Fargate with PostgreSQL RLS for tenant isolation and Redis caching for sub-5ms decisions.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: Gin middleware chain validates per-tenant JWT, resolves tenant_context, evaluates ABAC policy tree in-process, emits audit_events append-only to PostgreSQL.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: SOC 2 CC6/CC7/CC9 controls; JWKS rotation; PostgreSQL RLS; append-only audit trigger; GDPR residency enforcement at ALB layer.

target_output.thinking_summary: Addressing two high-severity issues in round 3: (1) routing compliance report queries to a dedicated Aurora read replica to eliminate write-path contention, and (2) fixing the Redis cache key to include sha256(tenant_context_id) as a prefix to prevent cross-tenant decision bleed. Both fixes are surgical changes to the deployment topology and the policy_engine cache key construction, leaving the core ABAC evaluation architecture intact.

fix_report.action_taken: Added dedicated Aurora read replica endpoint to deployment topology; compliance report generation path now exclusively uses the replica connection string injected via COMPLIANCE_DB_READER_URL env var; primary endpoint reserved for evaluation write path
fix_report.expected_outcome: Nightly compliance report batch queries no longer contend with real-time audit_events writes; p99 evaluation latency returns to baseline during report generation windows

fix_report.action_taken: Updated Redis cache key format to sha256(tenant_context_id)+':'+principal_identity_id+':'+resource_target+':'+action in policy_engine cache layer; added unit test asserting Tenant A decision is never returned for Tenant B cache lookup
fix_report.expected_outcome: Cross-tenant cache collision eliminated; cache hit rate unaffected since tenant_context_id is stable per request context

target_output.title: Enterprise Authorization Policy Service
target_output.executive_summary: A Go-based RBAC/ABAC authorization API service running on AWS ECS Fargate that evaluates access decisions in under 5ms for enterprise SaaS tenants. Policy rules, role assignments, and permission scopes are stored in PostgreSQL 16 with row-level security enforcing per-tenant boundaries. Redis 7 memoizes evaluation decisions with tenant-scoped cache keys. An append-only audit_events table feeds nightly compliance reports shipped to S3 for SOC 2 and ISO 27001 evidence packages.
target_output.architecture_overview: Request arrives at ALB with a tenant-signed JWT. Gin middleware validates the token against the tenant's JWKS endpoint, injects the resolved tenant_context into the request context, and routes to the policy evaluation handler. The in-process ABAC evaluator resolves matching policy_rules from a Redis-cached policy graph, applies RBAC role_assignments for the principal_identity, and returns an allow/deny decision with matched policy ID. The decision and full evaluation context are written asynchronously to the append-only audit_events partition. Compliance reports are generated by a nightly Prefect job reading exclusively from a dedicated Aurora read replica, isolating batch analytics from the real-time write path. Redis cache keys are prefixed with sha256(tenant_context_id) to enforce tenant isolation at the cache layer.
target_output.technology_stack: Backend: Go 1.22, Gin, custom ABAC evaluation engine, go-jose for JWT/JWKS. Data: Aurora PostgreSQL 16 (primary + read replica) with PgBouncer, Redis 7 ElastiCache cluster (3 shards). Infrastructure: ECS Fargate (us-east-1a/1c/1d), ALB, ECR, S3 for compliance archives, Secrets Manager for JWKS signing keys. CI/CD: GitHub Actions with golangci-lint, go test -race, k6 load tests. Observability: Datadog APM with evaluation trace spans.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Policy Evaluation Engine: accepts POST /v1/evaluate with (principal, resource, action, context attributes), resolves matching ABAC policies for the caller's tenant_context, returns allow/deny with policy_matched_id and evaluation_trace. Role Management: CRUD for role_assignments and permission_scopes per tenant. Policy Administration: CRUD for policy_rules with priority ordering and effect precedence. Audit Stream: paginated GET /v1/audit-events with filtering by principal, resource, decision, and time range. Compliance Reporting: async POST /v1/compliance-reports generating SOC 2 evidence packages from read-replica data.
target_output.system_components: ALB with WAF (rate limiting 1000 RPM per tenant); ECS Fargate cluster (2-10 tasks, auto-scale on p95 eval latency); Aurora PostgreSQL 16 primary (evaluation writes, policy reads) + read replica (compliance report queries); PgBouncer sidecar (50 connections per Fargate task); Redis 7 ElastiCache cluster (policy decision cache, 60s TTL, tenant-scoped keys); S3 bucket (compliance report archives with SSE-KMS); Secrets Manager (per-tenant JWKS signing keys with 24h rotation).
target_output.workflows: Authorization Decision Flow: SaaS backend sends POST /v1/evaluate with JWT and input attributes. Gin middleware verifies JWT signature against tenant JWKS, extracts tenant_context_id, checks Redis for cached decision using key sha256(tenant_context_id)+':'+principal+':'+resource+':'+action. On cache miss, evaluator loads policy_rules from PostgreSQL filtered by tenant_context_id RLS, traverses ABAC attribute conditions, resolves RBAC role_assignments, returns first matching allow/deny with priority order. Decision written to audit_events asynchronously via buffered channel. Response returned in < 5ms P99. Compliance Report Generation: Prefect job fires nightly at 02:00 UTC, connects exclusively to Aurora read replica, aggregates audit_events for the past 30 days by tenant and decision outcome, serialises to signed JSON report, uploads to S3 with SHA-256 manifest, sends download link to tenant admin via SES.
target_output.data_model: policy_rules (id UUID PK, tenant_context_id UUID FK NOT NULL, rule_type ENUM rbac/abac, subject_expr JSONB, resource_expr JSONB, action_expr TEXT[], effect ENUM allow/deny NOT NULL, priority INT, created_by UUID, deleted_at TIMESTAMP). role_assignments (id UUID PK, tenant_context_id UUID FK NOT NULL, principal_identity_id UUID FK NOT NULL, role_name TEXT, permission_scopes UUID[] FK, granted_by UUID, expires_at TIMESTAMP). permission_scopes (id UUID PK, tenant_context_id UUID FK NOT NULL, scope_name TEXT, actions TEXT[]). audit_events (id UUID PK, tenant_context_id UUID FK NOT NULL, principal_identity_id UUID, resource_target TEXT, action TEXT, decision ENUM allow/deny, policy_matched_id UUID, evaluation_ms INT, evaluated_at TIMESTAMPTZ, CONSTRAINT no_update CHECK(true)) PARTITIONED BY RANGE (evaluated_at). tenant_contexts (id UUID PK, jwks_url TEXT, encryption_key_arn TEXT, tier TEXT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). principal_identities (id UUID PK, tenant_context_id UUID FK NOT NULL, external_sub TEXT, attribute_claims JSONB, last_seen_at TIMESTAMPTZ). resource_targets (id UUID PK, tenant_context_id UUID FK NOT NULL, resource_type TEXT, resource_path TEXT).
target_output.api_design: All routes under /v1 require Authorization: Bearer <tenant-JWT>. POST /v1/evaluate {principal_id, resource, action, attributes} -> {decision, policy_matched_id, evaluation_trace, evaluated_at}. GET /v1/policies?page=1&limit=50 -> paginated policy_rules. POST /v1/policies {rule_type, subject_expr, resource_expr, action_expr, effect, priority}. DELETE /v1/policies/:id (soft-delete). GET /v1/roles -> role_assignments for caller's tenant. POST /v1/role-assignments {principal_identity_id, role_name, expires_at}. GET /v1/audit-events?principal=&resource=&decision=&from=&to= (read replica). POST /v1/compliance-reports {period_start, period_end} -> 202 Accepted with job_id. Rate limit: 2000 RPM per tenant enforced at ALB WAF.
target_output.security_and_compliance: SOC 2 CC6.1: per-tenant JWKS rotation every 24 hours via Secrets Manager scheduled rotation; RS256 tokens with 15-minute TTL. CC6.6: PostgreSQL RLS policy `(tenant_context_id = current_setting('app.tenant_id')::uuid)` on all tables; Fargate task role has no cross-tenant DB access. CC7.2: audit_events table has PostgreSQL trigger raising exception on any UPDATE or DELETE attempt; append-only enforced at DB level. CC9.2: Redis cache keys prefixed with sha256(tenant_context_id) preventing cross-tenant decision bleed. ISO 27001 A.10: AES-256 SSE-KMS for S3 compliance archives; Aurora storage encryption with CMK. GDPR Article 32: all data confined to us-east-1; ALB geo-restriction rule blocks non-US originating management requests.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: ECS Fargate cluster in us-east-1a/1c/1d with 2 minimum tasks scaling to 10 on p95 evaluation latency > 3ms. Aurora PostgreSQL 16 Multi-AZ primary (evaluation writes and policy reads) plus dedicated read replica (compliance report queries only, connection string COMPLIANCE_DB_READER_URL injected at task definition level). Redis 7 ElastiCache cluster mode with 3 shards and in-transit encryption. GitHub Actions pipeline: golangci-lint with custom ABAC rule checks, go test -race, k6 load test at 10K RPS blocking deploy on p99 > 5ms regression, Docker build with ko, ECR push, ECS canary deploy 10% traffic shift, automated rollback if p99 alarm breaches 8ms within 5 minutes of deploy. Secrets rotation: Secrets Manager rotates JWKS signing keys every 24 hours with a Lambda rotation function; zero-downtime overlap period serves old and new keys simultaneously.
target_output.observability: Datadog APM: trace on every /v1/evaluate request with spans for JWT validation, RLS policy resolution, ABAC tree traversal, Redis cache lookup, and audit write. Custom metrics: eval_latency_ms (histogram, P50/P95/P99), policy_cache_hit_ratio (target >85%), audit_write_lag_ms (alert if >100ms), active_tenant_request_rate per tenant_context_id. PagerDuty alerts: p99 eval_latency_ms > 8ms (critical), cache hit ratio < 70% (warning), audit_write_lag > 500ms (critical), Aurora replica lag > 60s (warning during nightly batch window).
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Baseline: 2 Fargate tasks (0.5 vCPU, 1GB) + Aurora db.r6g.large + Redis cache.r6g.large cluster = ~$680/month. At 10K RPS peak: auto-scale to 8 tasks; Aurora primary handles write-only load; read replica absorbs batch queries. Reserved Fargate baseline saves 30%. Redis node right-sizing after 30-day cache hit rate analysis.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 (6 weeks): Core policy evaluation engine (RBAC path), JWT/JWKS auth middleware, PostgreSQL schema with RLS, audit_events append-only table, POST /v1/evaluate endpoint, GitHub Actions CI with k6 load gate. Phase 2 (5 weeks): ABAC attribute evaluation, full policy CRUD API, role_assignments management, Redis cache layer with tenant-scoped keys (ISS-002 fix), Datadog APM integration. Phase 3 (5 weeks): Aurora read replica provisioning for compliance reports (ISS-001 fix), compliance report generation Prefect job, S3 archive pipeline, SOC 2 evidence export endpoint, ISO 27001 audit package tooling.
target_output.development_guidelines: Go modules with explicit dependency pinning. golangci-lint with staticcheck and gosec enabled in CI. All cache key construction centralised in pkg/cache/keys.go; key format changes require PR review from security team. ABAC evaluation logic 100% unit-tested with table-driven test cases covering every policy rule type. k6 load test must pass P99 <= 5ms gate before any deploy to production. ADRs required for changes to RLS policies or audit_events table schema.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: ABAC policy tree complexity grows quadratically with attribute count per tenant; mitigation: cap attribute cardinality per policy_rule at 10 and add evaluation timeout circuit breaker at 4ms returning deny-safe default. Risk: Aurora read replica replication lag during heavy write periods could serve stale policy data to compliance reports; mitigation: compliance job checks replica lag < 30s before starting batch query. Tradeoff: in-process policy evaluation couples engine updates to service deployments vs OPA sidecar approach; accepted for latency SLA but engine interface is abstracted behind an Evaluator interface for future swap.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Selected Aurora read replica over separate RDS instance for compliance report isolation to reuse existing PostgreSQL schema and RLS policies. Confirmed sha256 prefix strategy for Redis tenant isolation over namespace-per-tenant Redis DB approach (avoided due to Redis cluster mode DB index limitations). Confirmed append-only constraint via PostgreSQL trigger over application-layer enforcement for tamper-proof audit compliance.

metadata.notes: Round 3 fixes two high-severity issues: read replica isolation for compliance queries and tenant-scoped Redis cache keys.

project_goal.value: Continuously synchronise user identities and group memberships from Workday to Okta and Azure AD via SCIM 2.0, reconciling provisioning drift and managing deprovisioning on offboarding events
project_goal.rationale: Core automation requirement replacing manual HR-to-IdP sync

target_users.value: HR operations team, IT identity administrators, and downstream IdP consumers (Okta, Azure AD)
target_users.rationale: Primary operators and consuming systems identified in discovery

project_class.rationale: Headless automation with batch jobs and REST override API, no UI required

capabilities.rationale: Covers SCIM sync engine, persistence, IdP connectors, scheduled reconciliation, and override REST API

complexity_level.rationale: Multi-IdP SCIM orchestration with conflict resolution and audit trail complexity

risk_level.rationale: Processes PII and controls access to all downstream systems; a bug causes mass deprovisioning or access retention

data_sensitivity.rationale: Syncs employee PII including names, emails, job titles, and department memberships

external_exposure.rationale: Exposes REST override API to HR system and IdP partners via OAuth 2.0 client credentials

access_model.value: OAuth 2.0 client credentials per partner, scoped tokens per capability (read:identities, write:provisions, admin:overrides)
access_model.rationale: Least-privilege partner access enforced per integration

feature_scope.value: SCIM 2.0 sync from Workday to Okta and Azure AD, drift reconciliation, offboarding deprovision within SLA, manual override REST API, audit event stream
feature_scope.rationale: Full feature set identified in requirements

mvp_scope.value: Workday-to-Okta SCIM sync with offboarding deprovisioning and audit log; Azure AD connector in phase 2
mvp_scope.rationale: Okta integration highest priority for Q2 pilot

security_baseline.value: mTLS between service and IdP connectors, OAuth 2.0 client credentials for override API, secrets in Azure Key Vault, PII masked in logs
security_baseline.rationale: Partner-facing API and PII handling require strong transport and credential controls

frontend_stack.value: none - headless automation service
frontend_stack.rationale: No user-facing UI; operators use REST API and monitoring dashboards

backend_stack.rationale: Team expertise; Fastify well-suited for low-overhead API and async SCIM operations

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 on Azure Database for PostgreSQL, Redis 7 for idempotency and job state
data_platform.rationale: Relational for identity records and audit trail; Redis for dedup and distributed locking

hosting_target.value: Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) with Workload Identity
hosting_target.rationale: Existing AKS cluster, Workload Identity eliminates credential management for Azure AD connector

privacy_retention_policy.value: Identity sync logs retained 2 years; deprovisioning audit events retained 7 years; PII purged 90 days after offboarding
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Regulatory and internal retention obligations for employee data

future_scope.value: Google Workspace connector, SAP SuccessFactors as secondary HR source, self-service provisioning request portal
future_scope.rationale: Year 2 roadmap pending pilot success

constraints.value: Must complete Workday-triggered deprovisioning within 15 minutes of termination event; no persistent plaintext PII in logs
constraints.rationale: SLA contractually defined; log PII exposure is policy violation

observability_baseline.value: Datadog APM with distributed traces across sync jobs, structured JSON logs, custom metrics for provisioning lag and drift count, PagerDuty alert on deprovisioning SLA breach
observability_baseline.rationale: Corporate observability standard; SLA breach requires immediate alerting

execution_preference.value: Iterative delivery with 2-week sprints; Okta connector first, Azure AD second
execution_preference.rationale: Allows early pilot validation with primary IdP before expanding

compliance_context.value: SOC 2 Type II controls for access provisioning, ISO 27001 A.7 HR security, GDPR data minimisation and right-to-erasure for employee PII
compliance_context.rationale: SOC 2 audit scope includes identity provisioning; GDPR applies to EU employee data

project.goal: Automate Workday-to-IdP identity sync with deprovisioning SLA enforcement
project.target_users: HR ops, IT identity admins
project.timeline: Q2 Okta pilot, Q3 Azure AD

security.auth: OAuth 2.0 client credentials for partner API, Workload Identity for AKS
security.transport: mTLS to IdP connectors, TLS 1.3 on override API

data.db: PostgreSQL 16 on Azure Database for PostgreSQL
data.cache: Redis 7 for Bull job queues, idempotency keys, and reconciliation cursors

reasoner_reviews.product: The 15-minute deprovisioning SLA is the critical non-functional requirement. Any architecture that buffers termination events in slow-polling batch jobs risks SLA breach. Event-driven processing from Workday webhooks is essential for MVP.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Fastify with Bull job queue on Redis provides the event-driven pipeline needed. SCIM 2.0 pagination for the reconciliation job must handle Workday tenants with 50,000+ employees without memory exhaustion. Consider connection pooling limits on the Azure Database for PostgreSQL instance under peak reconciliation load.
reasoner_reviews.security: mTLS between the sync service and Okta/Azure AD SCIM endpoints prevents token interception. The override API must enforce scoped OAuth 2.0 tokens with per-partner client IDs. PII masking in structured logs is non-negotiable for SOC 2.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: The 15-minute deprovisioning window requires webhook delivery from Workday with a retry budget. Redis-backed idempotency keys prevent duplicate deprovisioning on webhook retries. Bull job priority queues must prioritise termination events over routine sync.
reasoner_reviews.critic: Drift reconciliation touching 50,000+ identities nightly could overwhelm the PostgreSQL write path. Recommend batched upserts with ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE and a pagination cursor stored in Redis to allow resumable reconciliation after failure.

backend.service_design: Three async workers: ProvisioningWorker (handles Workday webhook events, maps SCIM payloads, calls IdP SCIM endpoints), ReconciliationWorker (nightly drift scan with cursor-based pagination), OverrideAPIWorker (processes manual override actions). Fastify HTTP server exposes the override REST API and Workday webhook receiver.
backend.api_patterns: REST override API at /v1/identities/:id/deprovision, /v1/identities/:id/provision, /v1/sync/status, /v1/drift-report. Workday webhook receiver at /webhooks/workday (HMAC-SHA256 signature validation). OpenAPI 3.1 spec published for partner integration.
backend.business_modules: ScimMapper (transforms Workday profile schema to SCIM 2.0 User/Group payloads), IdpConnector (Okta and Azure AD SCIM client with retry and circuit breaker), DriftReconciler (compares PostgreSQL identity roster against live IdP state), AuditEmitter (appends immutable audit events).
backend.background_jobs: Bull queues: high-priority 'offboarding' queue (max concurrency 10, SLA monitored), standard 'provisioning' queue (new hires, updates), nightly 'reconciliation' queue (cursor-resumed). Job progress persisted in PostgreSQL.
backend.failure_handling: Webhook delivery retried by Workday up to 3 times; idempotency key in Redis prevents duplicate processing. SCIM call failures retried with exponential backoff up to 5 attempts; unresolved failures escalate to PagerDuty.

security.auth_design: OAuth 2.0 client credentials flow for override REST API; Azure Workload Identity for AKS pod-to-Azure AD SCIM; Workday webhook HMAC-SHA256 signature validation per event
security.authorization_model: Scoped tokens: read:identities, write:provisions, admin:overrides. Token introspection at Fastify middleware layer.
security.secrets_management: Okta API token, Azure AD client secret, and Workday webhook secret stored in Azure Key Vault; mounted as environment variables via AKS SecretProviderClass with 15-minute cache TTL.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: Every provisioning and deprovisioning action written to audit_events with actor, target_identity_id, action_type, idp_target, timestamp, and outcome. PII fields replaced with masked tokens in application logs.
security.privacy_controls: Employee PII purged from identity_records 90 days after offboarding_completed_at; GDPR erasure requests trigger immediate nullification of name and email fields.

data.entities: identity_records (id UUID, workday_worker_id, email_masked, display_name_masked, department, job_title, status ENUM active/suspended/offboarded, okta_user_id, azure_ad_object_id, last_synced_at), group_memberships (id UUID, identity_id FK, group_external_id, idp_target ENUM okta/azure_ad, synced_at, removed_at), provisioning_events (id UUID, identity_id FK, event_type ENUM hire/update/terminate/manual_override, source ENUM workday/manual, idp_target, scim_operation, outcome, sla_seconds INT, processed_at), drift_reconciliations (id UUID, run_id, identity_id, drift_type ENUM stale_membership/missing_user/extra_user, resolved BOOL, detected_at), audit_events (id UUID, actor_client_id, action, target_identity_id, idp_target, outcome, ts TIMESTAMPTZ)
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL 16 with read replica for drift reports; Redis 7 for Bull job queues, idempotency keys (TTL 24h), and reconciliation cursors; Azure Blob Storage for 7-year audit event archive
data.retention_and_deletion: provisioning_events purged after 2 years; audit_events archived to Blob Storage after 90 days; identity_records PII nullified 90 days post-offboarding

devops.deployment_topology: AKS cluster with 3 namespaces (dev, staging, prod); Fastify API and worker pods deployed as separate Deployments with HPA; PostgreSQL on Azure Database Flexible Server with read replica; Redis 7 on Azure Cache for Redis Premium
devops.ci_cd_design: Azure DevOps Pipelines: lint (ESLint + TypeScript strict), unit tests (Vitest, 85% coverage gate), integration tests against Okta sandbox and Azure AD test tenant, Docker build and push to ACR, Helm chart deploy to AKS. Blue-green deploy for override API; rolling update for workers.
devops.observability_stack: Datadog APM with trace propagation across Bull jobs; custom metrics: provisioning_job_duration_ms, deprovision_sla_seconds, drift_count_by_type, idp_call_error_rate; PagerDuty alert when deprovision SLA > 14 minutes
devops.rollback_strategy: Helm rollback to previous chart revision on failed readiness probes; Bull jobs paused during rollback; database migrations are additive-only during rollout phase

target_output.thinking_summary: Designing a SCIM 2.0 identity sync automation with a hard 15-minute deprovisioning SLA. Key decisions: event-driven Bull queue with priority lanes for termination events over routine syncs, Redis idempotency keys to safely handle Workday webhook retries, AKS Workload Identity to eliminate credential management for Azure AD SCIM calls, and cursor-based reconciliation to handle 50,000+ employee rosters without memory exhaustion.
target_output.title: Workday-to-IdP SCIM Identity Sync Automation Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: A headless automation service that enforces real-time identity lifecycle management from Workday as the authoritative HR source to Okta and Azure AD via SCIM 2.0. Terminates employee access within a 15-minute SLA, reconciles provisioning drift nightly, and exposes a scoped REST override API for IT admin operations. Built on Node.js 20 with Fastify and Bull on AKS, with PostgreSQL 16 for identity state and Redis 7 for job orchestration.
target_output.architecture_overview: Event-driven automation platform on Azure Kubernetes Service. Workday fires signed webhook events to a Fastify receiver, which validates HMAC-SHA256 signatures and enqueues events into priority-stratified Bull queues backed by Redis 7. ProvisioningWorker pods process offboarding events with highest priority, calling Okta and Azure AD SCIM 2.0 endpoints to suspend or delete accounts within the 15-minute SLA window. A nightly ReconciliationWorker performs cursor-based comparison of the PostgreSQL identity roster against live IdP state, auto-remediating stale group memberships and raising alerts for unresolved drift. All provisioning and deprovisioning decisions are written to an immutable audit_events table, archived to Azure Blob Storage for 7-year retention.
target_output.technology_stack: Backend: Node.js 20, Fastify, Bull 4 (Redis-backed job queues), custom Okta and Azure AD SCIM 2.0 client adapters. Database: PostgreSQL 16 on Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server with read replica. Cache/Queue: Redis 7 on Azure Cache for Redis Premium. Auth: Azure Workload Identity for AKS pods, OAuth 2.0 client credentials for override API, Azure Key Vault via SecretProviderClass. CI/CD: Azure DevOps Pipelines with Helm chart deploys to AKS. Observability: Datadog APM, PagerDuty.
target_output.functional_feature_map: SCIM Sync Engine: maps Workday employee profile schema (worker_id, legal_name, department, cost_centre, employment_status) to SCIM 2.0 User and Group payloads for Okta and Azure AD. Offboarding Deprovisioning: processes Workday worker-terminated events within 15-minute SLA, suspending Okta user and disabling Azure AD account atomically. Group Membership Sync: propagates Workday organisation unit changes to IdP group memberships via SCIM PATCH /Groups. Drift Reconciliation: nightly cursor-based scan comparing PostgreSQL identity_records against Okta and Azure AD SCIM list responses, auto-remediating stale memberships. Override REST API: allows IT admins to force-deprovision, reactivate, or check sync status for specific identities with full audit trail.
target_output.system_components: Fastify Webhook Receiver (validates Workday HMAC-SHA256 signatures, enqueues to Bull), Bull Job Queue with priority lanes (offboarding > provisioning > group_sync > reconciliation), ProvisioningWorker pods (SCIM 2.0 calls to Okta and Azure AD with circuit breaker and retry), ReconciliationWorker pod (cursor-based nightly drift scan), Override REST API (Fastify, OAuth 2.0 scoped tokens, OpenAPI 3.1), PostgreSQL 16 (identity_records, provisioning_events, drift_reconciliations, audit_events), Redis 7 (Bull queues, idempotency keys TTL 24h, reconciliation cursor), Azure Key Vault (Okta token, Azure AD secret, Workday webhook secret), Datadog Agent sidecar.
target_output.workflows: Termination Event Deprovisioning: Workday fires worker-terminated webhook to /webhooks/workday; Fastify receiver validates HMAC-SHA256 signature, checks Redis idempotency key (24h TTL) to reject duplicate events, enqueues to high-priority offboarding Bull queue; ProvisioningWorker dequeues, calls Okta SCIM PATCH /Users/:id (active: false) and Azure AD SCIM DELETE /Users/:id with exponential retry-backoff on transient failures; outcome written to provisioning_events with sla_seconds measured from webhook receipt; PagerDuty fires if sla_seconds > 840. Nightly Drift Reconciliation: ReconciliationWorker wakes at 02:00 UTC, retrieves cursor from Redis (resumes from last position if crashed), paginates identity_records in 500-row batches, calls Okta and Azure AD SCIM Users list APIs, computes set difference for stale group memberships and missing or extra users, writes drift_reconciliations records, auto-remediates stale memberships via SCIM PATCH /Groups, raises alerts for missing users requiring human review.
target_output.data_model: identity_records (id UUID PK, workday_worker_id VARCHAR UNIQUE, email_masked VARCHAR, display_name_masked VARCHAR, department VARCHAR, job_title VARCHAR, status ENUM active/suspended/offboarded, okta_user_id VARCHAR, azure_ad_object_id VARCHAR, offboarding_completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ, last_synced_at TIMESTAMPTZ, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). group_memberships (id UUID PK, identity_id UUID FK, group_external_id VARCHAR, idp_target ENUM okta/azure_ad, synced_at TIMESTAMPTZ, removed_at TIMESTAMPTZ). provisioning_events (id UUID PK, identity_id UUID FK, event_type ENUM hire/update/terminate/manual_override, source ENUM workday/manual, idp_target ENUM okta/azure_ad, scim_operation VARCHAR, outcome ENUM success/failed/skipped, sla_seconds INT, processed_at TIMESTAMPTZ). drift_reconciliations (id UUID PK, run_id UUID, identity_id UUID FK, drift_type ENUM stale_membership/missing_user/extra_user, resolved BOOL, detected_at TIMESTAMPTZ). audit_events (id UUID PK, actor_client_id VARCHAR, action VARCHAR, target_identity_id UUID, idp_target ENUM okta/azure_ad, outcome VARCHAR, ts TIMESTAMPTZ). PII fields stored masked; raw values never written to logs or returned in API responses except to authorised override API callers.
target_output.api_design: REST Override API at /v1 (OpenAPI 3.1 spec). POST /v1/identities/:workday_worker_id/deprovision (force-deprovision, requires scope: write:provisions). POST /v1/identities/:workday_worker_id/reactivate (reactivate suspended account, scope: write:provisions). GET /v1/identities/:workday_worker_id/status (returns current provisioning state across Okta and Azure AD, scope: read:identities). GET /v1/sync/drift-report (paginated drift summary by drift_type and idp_target, scope: read:identities). POST /webhooks/workday (HMAC-SHA256 required in X-Workday-Signature header, no OAuth). GET /v1/healthz (unauthenticated liveness probe). All responses JSON with x-request-id header for trace correlation.
target_output.security_and_compliance: mTLS enforced between ProvisioningWorker and Okta and Azure AD SCIM endpoints using certificates managed by cert-manager on AKS. OAuth 2.0 client credentials with per-partner client IDs and scoped tokens for override REST API; Fastify token introspection middleware rejects expired or out-of-scope tokens. Workday webhook HMAC-SHA256 signature validated before any queue enqueue to prevent spoofed deprovision injection. Secrets stored in Azure Key Vault and mounted via AKS SecretProviderClass with 15-minute rotation cache; no static credentials in Kubernetes Secrets objects. PII fields replaced with SHA-256 masked tokens in all application logs; plaintext PII never written to stdout or log aggregator. SOC 2 Type II: audit_events table is INSERT-only in application layer; retained 90 days hot then archived to Azure Blob Storage for 7 years. GDPR: employee PII nullified in identity_records 90 days after offboarding_completed_at; Workday sync lock prevents re-population of purged records.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: AKS cluster with 3 namespaces: dev (single-replica), staging (multi-replica, Okta sandbox), production (multi-replica, HPA). Deployments: fastify-api (2-4 replicas, HPA on CPU 60%), provisioning-worker (3-8 replicas, HPA on Bull offboarding queue depth custom metric), reconciliation-worker (1 replica, CronJob trigger 02:00 UTC). Azure DevOps Pipelines: TypeScript strict + ESLint lint stage, Vitest unit tests (85% coverage gate), integration tests against Okta sandbox and Azure AD test tenant, Docker build pushed to Azure Container Registry, Helm chart deploy with blue-green for override API and rolling update for workers. Rollback: Helm rollback to previous chart revision on failed readiness probe; Bull queues paused during rollback window. Database migrations additive-only, applied via Kubernetes Job pre-deploy hook using Drizzle ORM migrate.
target_output.observability: Datadog APM with trace context propagated across Bull job lifecycle (enqueue -> dequeue -> SCIM call -> audit write). Custom metrics: provisioning_job_duration_ms histogram by job_type and idp_target, deprovision_sla_seconds gauge (P1 PagerDuty alert > 840s), drift_count_by_type by drift_type enum value, idp_scim_error_rate counter (P2 alert > 1%). Structured JSON logs with x-trace-id and x-request-id correlation; PII fields replaced with masked tokens before logging. PagerDuty P1 on SLA breach; P2 on SCIM error rate spike or reconciliation job crash.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: AKS worker auto-scaling handles Workday batch-hire events (quarter-start spikes of 500+ new hires) without manual intervention. PostgreSQL Flexible Server read replica offloads nightly drift-report queries from the write primary. Redis Premium tier ensures Bull queue availability during Azure maintenance events. Estimated ~$800 per month for a 10,000-employee tenant on a standard AKS node pool at moderate SCIM call volume.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (8 weeks): Workday webhook receiver with HMAC validation, ProvisioningWorker with Okta SCIM 2.0 connector, offboarding deprovisioning with 15-minute SLA monitoring, provisioning_events and audit_events PostgreSQL schema, Override REST API (deprovision and status endpoints), AKS deployment with Datadog APM and PagerDuty SLA alert. Phase 2 (6 weeks): Azure AD SCIM 2.0 connector, group membership sync propagation, nightly ReconciliationWorker with drift_reconciliations and cursor resume, GDPR PII purge scheduled job, drift-report API endpoint, read replica for reconciliation queries. Phase 3 (4 weeks): Google Workspace connector, self-service provisioning request portal integration hook, SOC 2 audit evidence export from audit_events archive.
target_output.development_guidelines: TypeScript strict mode throughout. ESLint + Prettier enforced in CI. Vitest for unit tests with ScimMapper, DriftReconciler, and AuditEmitter modules at 90% or higher coverage; integration tests use Okta sandbox tenant with recorded fixtures. Bull job handlers must be idempotent - always check Redis idempotency key before making SCIM API call. No plaintext PII in any log statement; use maskPii() utility wrapper before logging any identity field. ADR documents required for SCIM schema mapping decisions and conflict resolution policy.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Workday webhook delivery failures cause deprovisioning SLA breach if retry budget exhausted. Mitigation: Workday retries 3x with exponential backoff; Bull high-priority queue monitored by PagerDuty at 14-minute threshold leaving 1-minute response window before SLA breach. Risk: PostgreSQL write saturation during nightly reconciliation of 50,000 or more identities. Mitigation: cursor-based 500-row batching, read replica for Okta and Azure AD SCIM comparison queries, reconciliation paused during peak provisioning windows. Tradeoff: Redis idempotency keys with 24-hour TTL prevent duplicate deprovisioning on webhook retry but require Redis availability for correctness; mitigated by Azure Cache for Redis Premium with zone redundancy.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Selected Bull over AWS SQS for queue primitives given AKS and Azure hosting target. Confirmed Azure Workload Identity over service principal secrets for Azure AD SCIM connector to eliminate credential rotation toil. Chose cursor-based pagination over full-dataset snapshot for reconciliation to avoid memory exhaustion on large tenants. PII masking implemented at ScimMapper output layer, not database layer, to avoid double-masking on read paths.

metadata.notes: SCIM 2.0 identity sync automation with 15-min deprovisioning SLA, SOC 2 and GDPR controls on AKS.

project_goal.value: Migrate organisations from legacy shared-secret API keys to short-lived OAuth 2.0 access tokens, automatically rotating existing keys, updating downstream service configurations, and deprecating old credential endpoints over a phased rollout schedule.
project_goal.rationale: Eliminates long-lived static credentials that are a primary attack surface

target_users.value: Platform engineers and security teams managing internal service-to-service credential estates
target_users.rationale: Primary operators of the migration tooling

project_class.rationale: Orchestrated workflow tool with no browser UI, runs headlessly on EC2

capabilities.rationale: Prefect orchestration, external service configuration APIs, scheduled batches

complexity_level.rationale: Phased batch orchestration with external API calls; no real-time or ML components

risk_level.rationale: Touches live production credentials; a failed rotation can cause widespread service outages

data_sensitivity.rationale: Stores OAuth client secrets and service configurations during rotation

external_exposure.rationale: Runs on internal EC2, no public endpoints

access_model.value: IAM role-based access for EC2 execution; human operators authenticate via SSO for override actions
access_model.rationale: Principle of least privilege; automated flows use instance roles

feature_scope.value: Credential registry discovery, OAuth client provisioning, service configuration patching, deprecation milestone tracking, cutover verification, rollback support
feature_scope.rationale: Full lifecycle from discovery through decommission

mvp_scope.value: Discovery of legacy API keys, OAuth client provisioning, configuration patching for first 50 services, cutover confirmation via usage monitoring
mvp_scope.rationale: Validates core rotation workflow before scaling to full estate

security_baseline.value: OAuth client secrets stored in AWS Secrets Manager, all rotation actions logged to immutable audit trail, no plaintext credentials in code or logs
security_baseline.rationale: Zero-plaintext credential policy

frontend_stack.value: none — headless automation tool
frontend_stack.rationale: No UI; operators interact via CLI and Prefect dashboard

backend_stack.rationale: Prefect provides retry logic, task dependency graphs, and observability for multi-step batch jobs

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 for rotation state and audit records
data_platform.rationale: Relational model suits credential registry and rollout batch tracking

hosting_target.value: EC2 managed by Ansible in us-east-1
hosting_target.rationale: Existing Ansible estate; avoids Lambda cold-start for long-running rotation batches

privacy_retention_policy.value: Rotation audit logs retained 3 years; active OAuth client secrets purged within 24h of deprecation
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: SOC 2 evidence retention requirement

future_scope.value: Self-service rotation triggers for service owners, Slack approval workflow for high-impact services, support for GCP and Azure credential stores
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 roadmap after core migration complete

constraints.value: Must not cause service downtime during rotation; OAuth AS is internal Keycloak on-prem; Prefect Cloud not permitted — self-hosted Prefect server
constraints.rationale: Zero-downtime and data-residency constraints

observability_baseline.value: AWS CloudWatch for EC2 metrics and logs, Prefect UI for flow run status, structured JSON logs per rotation task
observability_baseline.rationale: Existing corporate monitoring stack

execution_preference.value: Weekly rotation batches per service cohort; manual trigger available for emergency rotations
execution_preference.rationale: Controlled rollout cadence minimises blast radius

compliance_context.value: SOC 2 Type II credential management controls, internal credential management policy, OAuth 2.0 RFC 6749
compliance_context.rationale: High risk_level and confidential data require compliance framing

project.goal: Automate phased migration of API key-based services to OAuth 2.0 tokens with zero downtime
project.target_users: Platform engineers and security team

backend.framework: Prefect for orchestration, SQLAlchemy for DB access
backend.api: No public API; Prefect deployment exposes internal management UI

security.auth: IAM instance role for automated flows; SSO for human overrides
security.secrets: AWS Secrets Manager; no plaintext secrets in code or environment
security.audit: Immutable rotation_events table with operator identity and timestamps

devops.ci: GitHub Actions for lint and unit tests

constraints.keycloak: self-hosted on-prem Keycloak as OAuth AS

reasoner_reviews.product: The phased rollout design (cohorts of 50 services per batch) is appropriate for blast-radius containment. The cutover verification step — monitoring token usage patterns before deactivating old keys — is a strong signal of operational maturity. Rollback capability must be explicit in the plan.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Prefect task graph suits the dependency chain: discover -> provision OAuth client -> patch config -> verify usage -> deactivate key. Consider idempotency keys on each Prefect task so retried flows do not double-provision OAuth clients. The PostgreSQL rotation_state table should track task-level status per service, not just batch-level.
reasoner_reviews.security: Storing OAuth client secrets transiently in Secrets Manager is correct. The rotation_events audit table must be append-only with no UPDATE/DELETE grants for the Prefect execution role. Ensure the Keycloak admin API calls use a dedicated service account with scoped Keycloak roles, not the master realm admin.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Zero-downtime constraint requires the old API key and new OAuth token to be simultaneously valid during the switchover window. Plan must explicitly define this dual-validity period and the mechanism for deactivating the old key only after usage drops to zero.

backend.service_design: Prefect flow per rotation cohort: tasks for credential_discovery, oauth_client_provisioning, config_patching, usage_verification, key_deactivation. Each task is idempotent using service_id as deduplication key.
backend.business_modules: CredentialRegistry module queries credential_registry table; KeycloakClient wraps admin REST API for client creation; ConfigPatcher dispatches per-service config update via each service's own configuration API; UsageMonitor polls CloudWatch API-call metrics to confirm token adoption.
backend.failure_handling: Prefect retry with exponential backoff on transient API failures. ConfigPatcher records patch_status per service; failed patches halt the cohort and page on-call. Rollback flow reactivates old API key via Keycloak client disable toggle.
backend.background_jobs: Nightly deprecation_sweep flow scans for services past their cutover deadline with non-zero old-key usage and escalates to the service owner via email.

security.auth_design: EC2 instance profile with least-privilege IAM policy: secretsmanager:GetSecretValue scoped to rotation/* prefix, cloudwatch:GetMetricData for usage verification. Keycloak admin calls use a dedicated rotation-bot service account with realm-scoped client management permissions only.
security.secrets_management: Freshly provisioned OAuth client secrets written to Secrets Manager under rotation/{service_id}/oauth_client. Secrets tagged with rotation_batch_id for lifecycle tracking. Old API key records marked deprecated but not deleted until post-cutover grace period.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: rotation_events table (id, service_id, action ENUM discover/provision/patch/verify/deactivate/rollback, operator_id, batch_id, outcome, detail_json, created_at) with INSERT-only grant to Prefect role. CloudWatch log group /credential-migration with 3-year retention.

data.entities: legacy_api_keys (id, service_id, key_hash, status ENUM active/deprecated/deactivated, discovered_at, deactivated_at), oauth_clients (id, service_id, client_id, secret_sm_path, keycloak_realm, issued_at, status), rotation_schedules (id, service_id, cohort_id, scheduled_rotation_date, cutover_date, status ENUM pending/in_progress/verified/complete/rolled_back), rotation_events (id, service_id, action, operator_id, batch_id, outcome, detail_json, created_at), rollout_batches (id, batch_name, cohort_size, start_date, status)
data.storage_design: RDS PostgreSQL 16 in us-east-1 with Multi-AZ standby. Prefect backend database shares the same RDS instance on a separate schema. Secrets Manager for transient credential storage.
data.migration_notes: Alembic migrations run as a Prefect pre-flow task before each batch to ensure schema is current. Migration history table tracks applied versions.

devops.deployment_topology: Prefect server and agent run on two separate EC2 t3.medium instances provisioned by Ansible. Prefect agent polls server for deployments. PostgreSQL on RDS Multi-AZ. GitHub Actions builds and pushes Prefect flow source to S3; Ansible pulls and registers new flow versions.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: flake8 lint, pytest unit suite (mocked Keycloak and Secrets Manager), integration test against local Keycloak Docker container. Deployments trigger Ansible playbook via GitHub Actions self-hosted runner.
devops.rollback_strategy: Each rotation batch records pre-patch service config snapshots to S3. Rollback Prefect flow restores snapshot and reactivates original API key. Rollback window: 7 days post-cutover.
devops.observability_stack: CloudWatch for EC2 and RDS metrics. Prefect UI for flow run history and task logs. CloudWatch alarms: Prefect agent heartbeat, RDS connection count, Secrets Manager API error rate.

ISS-001.title: Dual-validity period not specified in plan
ISS-001.detail: Round 1 plan did not define how long both the old API key and new OAuth token remain simultaneously valid, nor the mechanism for automatic old-key deactivation once usage metrics confirm zero old-key calls.

history.note: Identified during security review: missing dual-validity window definition

ISS-002.title: Prefect task idempotency not addressed
ISS-002.detail: If a Prefect flow is retried after partial completion, oauth_client_provisioning may attempt to create a duplicate Keycloak client for an already-provisioned service. No deduplication key or existence-check logic was present.

history.note: Architect reviewer flagged missing idempotency on provisioning task

focus_issues.title: Dual-validity period not specified in plan
focus_issues.detail: Round 1 plan did not define how long both the old API key and new OAuth token remain simultaneously valid, nor the mechanism for automatic old-key deactivation once usage metrics confirm zero old-key calls.

focus_issues.title: Prefect task idempotency not addressed
focus_issues.detail: If a Prefect flow is retried after partial completion, oauth_client_provisioning may attempt to create a duplicate Keycloak client for an already-provisioned service. No deduplication key or existence-check logic was present.

latest_recommendations: Define explicit dual-validity window with automated monitoring-based deactivation trigger
Add idempotency check in oauth_client_provisioning task using service_id as deduplication key
revision_memory.latest_plan_title: API Key to OAuth 2.0 Migration Automation — Round 1

convergence_state.reason: Two unresolved issues including one high-severity

previous_audits.summary: Core orchestration design is sound but the plan omits critical operational safety details: the dual-validity switchover window is undefined and Prefect task idempotency is unaddressed, both of which create outage risk in production.
strengths: Prefect task graph correctly models the discovery-to-deactivation lifecycle
Secrets Manager integration for transient credential storage is appropriate
Rollout cohort design limits blast radius
concerns: No definition of how long old API keys and new OAuth tokens coexist
Retry scenarios could create duplicate Keycloak clients
blocking_issues: Dual-validity window undefined — service outage risk if old key deactivated before OAuth adoption confirmed
recommendations: Specify dual-validity period (e.g., 14 days) and define the CloudWatch metric threshold (zero old-key calls over 24h) that triggers automatic deactivation
Implement idempotency in oauth_client_provisioning: query Keycloak for existing client by service_id before creating

issue_updates.title: Dual-validity period not specified
issue_updates.detail: Deactivation logic missing; risk of premature key removal

issue_updates.title: Prefect task idempotency not addressed
issue_updates.detail: Duplicate client creation possible on retry

previous_plan.title: API Key to OAuth 2.0 Migration Automation — Round 1
previous_plan.executive_summary: A Prefect-orchestrated Python tool running on EC2 that discovers legacy API key usages across the internal service estate, provisions OAuth 2.0 clients in Keycloak, patches each service configuration, and deactivates old keys after confirming successful token adoption.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: Prefect self-hosted server and agent on EC2 orchestrate a task graph: discovery queries the credential_registry table, provisioning calls Keycloak admin API, patching calls each service config API, verification polls CloudWatch usage metrics, and deactivation disables the old key record.
previous_plan.workflows: Discovery scans credential_registry for active API keys; provisioning creates Keycloak OAuth clients; config patching updates service environment configs; deactivation marks keys deprecated.
previous_plan.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: premature old-key deactivation causes service auth failures. Risk: retried Prefect tasks create duplicate Keycloak clients. Mitigation strategies not yet specified.

target_output.thinking_summary: This is a round 2 revision addressing two issues from the first pass: undefined dual-validity window (ISS-001) and missing Prefect task idempotency (ISS-002). The plan must explicitly define the 14-day coexistence window, the CloudWatch metric threshold for safe deactivation, and the existence-check pattern in the provisioning task. Plan quality is weak per manifest — sections should be present but kept thinner, reflecting a plan that resolves the issues but still has some underdeveloped areas.

fix_report.action_taken: Added explicit dual-validity window definition: old API key and new OAuth token coexist for 14 days post-patch. Deactivation task now requires 24 consecutive hours of zero CloudWatch API-Gateway calls using the old key_id before proceeding. Updated workflows and deployment_and_operations sections.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Zero-downtime guarantee: old key is only deactivated after empirical usage evidence confirms full token adoption

fix_report.action_taken: Added idempotency check to oauth_client_provisioning task: task queries Keycloak admin API for existing client matching service_id label before creating; if found, task records existing client_id and proceeds without creation. Updated architecture_overview and system_components.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Idempotent retries: repeated Prefect task execution produces the same state without duplicating Keycloak clients

target_output.title: API Key to OAuth 2.0 Migration Automation
target_output.executive_summary: A Prefect-orchestrated Python automation tool on EC2 that migrates internal services from shared-secret API keys to short-lived OAuth 2.0 tokens via a five-stage task graph: discovery, idempotent Keycloak client provisioning, service configuration patching, usage-metric-gated deactivation, and rollback. Old API keys and new tokens coexist for a 14-day dual-validity window; deactivation requires 24 consecutive hours of zero old-key usage in CloudWatch.
target_output.architecture_overview: Python 3.12 Prefect flows execute on an EC2 agent, pulling flow code from S3. Each rotation cohort runs a Prefect deployment with five tasks: (1) CredentialDiscovery queries the credential_registry PostgreSQL table for services with active API keys in the cohort; (2) OAuthClientProvisioner idempotently creates Keycloak OAuth 2.0 clients, checking for existing clients by service_id label before creation; (3) ConfigPatcher calls each service's configuration API to inject the new client_id and secret_sm_path; (4) UsageVerifier polls CloudWatch API-Gateway metrics for 24 consecutive hours of zero old-key calls before signalling safe deactivation; (5) KeyDeactivator marks the legacy_api_keys record as deactivated in PostgreSQL and disables the old key in the credential store. Dual-validity window is 14 days from patch completion.
target_output.technology_stack: Python 3.12, Prefect 2.x (self-hosted server and agent), SQLAlchemy 2.0 with Alembic, PostgreSQL 16 on RDS Multi-AZ, AWS Secrets Manager, AWS CloudWatch Metrics API, Keycloak Admin REST API, boto3, GitHub Actions for CI, Ansible for EC2 provisioning. pytest with moto for AWS Secrets Manager mocking, pytest-postgresql for Alembic migration testing. structlog for JSON-structured audit logs shipped to CloudWatch Logs Insights.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Credential discovery: scans credential_registry for active API keys grouped by rollout_batch. OAuth provisioning: idempotent Keycloak client creation with service_id label deduplication. Config patching: per-service configuration API update injecting OAuth credentials. Dual-validity monitoring: CloudWatch-based usage gate enforcing 24h zero-old-key-call requirement. Key deactivation: database record update and credential store disable. Emergency rollback: S3 snapshot restore and old key reactivation. Nightly deprecation sweep: escalation emails for overdue cohorts.
target_output.system_components: Prefect Server (EC2 t3.medium): flow scheduling and run history. Prefect Agent (EC2 t3.medium): task execution. PostgreSQL RDS Multi-AZ: credential_registry, rotation_schedules, rotation_events, rollout_batches tables. AWS Secrets Manager: transient OAuth client secret storage under rotation/{service_id}/oauth_client. Keycloak on-prem: OAuth 2.0 authorization server. CloudWatch: API-Gateway usage metrics for deactivation gating. S3: pre-patch config snapshots for rollback, Prefect flow source bundles.
target_output.workflows: Rotation Batch Flow: Prefect deployment triggered weekly per cohort or manually for emergency. CredentialDiscovery selects services in batch with status=pending. OAuthClientProvisioner calls GET /admin/realms/{realm}/clients?q=service_id:{id} first; if client exists records client_id, skips creation; otherwise creates client and writes secret to Secrets Manager. ConfigPatcher calls each service config API with new client_id and secret_sm_path, records patch_status. Dual-validity window starts: both old key and new token are valid. UsageVerifier polls CloudWatch GetMetricData every hour for old_key_api_calls metric; after 24 consecutive zero-call hours KeyDeactivator fires, sets legacy_api_keys.status=deactivated, inserts rotation_events record. Rollback Flow: fetches S3 config snapshot for service, restores via config API, calls Keycloak to disable OAuth client, reactivates old API key record. Nightly sweep flow queries services past cutover_date with status != complete and emails service owners.
target_output.data_model: legacy_api_keys (id UUID PK, service_id, key_hash SHA256, credential_store_ref, status ENUM active/deprecated/deactivated, discovered_at, deactivated_at). oauth_clients (id UUID PK, service_id, keycloak_client_id, secret_sm_path, keycloak_realm, issued_at, status ENUM active/revoked). rotation_schedules (id UUID PK, service_id, cohort_id FK rollout_batches, scheduled_rotation_date, cutover_date, dual_validity_end_date, status ENUM pending/in_progress/verified/complete/rolled_back). rotation_events (id UUID PK, service_id, action ENUM discover/provision/patch/verify/deactivate/rollback, operator_id, batch_id, outcome ENUM success/failure, detail_json, created_at — INSERT only). rollout_batches (id UUID PK, batch_name, cohort_size, start_date, status).
target_output.api_design: No public HTTP API. Internal Prefect Python API: run_rotation_batch(batch_id: str) -> FlowRun, trigger_emergency_rotation(service_id: str) -> FlowRun, trigger_rollback(service_id: str) -> FlowRun. Keycloak Admin REST consumed: GET /admin/realms/{realm}/clients?q=service_id:{id}, POST /admin/realms/{realm}/clients, GET /admin/realms/{realm}/clients/{id}/client-secret. CloudWatch consumed: GetMetricData for APIGateway/Count filtered by old key_id.
target_output.security_and_compliance: IAM instance profile with secretsmanager:GetSecretValue scoped to arn:aws:secretsmanager:us-east-1:*:secret:rotation/*. Keycloak rotation-bot service account with realm-level client management role only — no realm admin. rotation_events table has INSERT-only grant for Prefect execution role; no UPDATE or DELETE. All Prefect task logs redact secret values via a custom log filter. CloudWatch log group /credential-migration with 3-year retention for SOC 2 evidence. OAuth client secrets purged from Secrets Manager 24h after successful deactivation. SOC 2 Type II and RFC 6749 compliance documented in rotation runbook.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Two EC2 t3.medium instances provisioned by Ansible: prefect-server and prefect-agent. GitHub Actions builds flow source zip and uploads to S3 on merge to main; Ansible pull triggered via GitHub Actions self-hosted runner registers new Prefect deployment version. RDS PostgreSQL Multi-AZ in us-east-1 with automated backups and 7-day retention. Environments: dev (single EC2, local PostgreSQL Docker), staging (mirrors prod with non-production Keycloak realm), production. Alembic migrations run as a Prefect pre-flow hook before first task. Ansible playbook handles EC2 patching in rolling fashion — agent first, then server.
target_output.observability: CloudWatch alarms: Prefect agent heartbeat missing > 5 min (page on-call), RDS connection count > 80% (warn), Secrets Manager API error rate > 1% (warn). Prefect UI: flow run history, task state timeline, retry counts per task. Structured JSON logs per task including service_id, batch_id, action, outcome. CloudWatch metric dashboard: rotation_batches_completed_week, old_key_deactivations_today, services_in_dual_validity_window.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Two EC2 t3.medium instances ~$60/month. RDS db.t3.medium Multi-AZ ~$120/month. Secrets Manager ~$0.40 per secret per month — peak 500 secrets during migration = $200. Total ~$380/month. Scales linearly with cohort size; no architectural change needed up to 2,000 services.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (4 weeks): Prefect server and agent setup, credential_discovery and oauth_client_provisioning tasks, PostgreSQL schema and Alembic migrations, Keycloak integration tests, rotation of first 50 services in staging. Phase 2 (3 weeks): config_patching task for all service config API types, dual-validity monitoring with CloudWatch gates, key_deactivation task, production rollout of first cohort. Phase 3 (3 weeks): rollback flow, nightly deprecation sweep, emergency rotation trigger, full estate rollout.
target_output.development_guidelines: All Prefect tasks must be decorated with @task(retries=3, retry_delay_seconds=60). Idempotency enforced: every provisioning task checks existence before create. No credentials logged — custom Prefect log filter strips secret_sm_path values. Unit tests mock Keycloak admin API and Secrets Manager with moto and responses libraries. Integration tests run against local Keycloak Docker container in GitHub Actions.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: CloudWatch metric delay (up to 5 min lag) could allow a brief window where old-key calls are in flight when deactivation fires. Mitigation: extend zero-call requirement to 24 consecutive hours, not just the last data point. Risk: Keycloak admin API rate limiting during large cohort provisioning. Mitigation: add 100ms inter-call sleep and exponential backoff. Tradeoff: self-hosted Prefect over managed Prefect Cloud adds operational burden but satisfies data-residency constraint.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: ISS-001 resolved: dual-validity window set to 14 days; deactivation gated on 24 consecutive hours of zero CloudWatch old-key-call metrics. ISS-002 resolved: OAuthClientProvisioner checks for existing Keycloak client by service_id label before creation, making the task safe to retry. Emergency rotation bypass available for critical security incidents.

metadata.notes: Round 2 revision of API key to OAuth migration tool; fixes dual-validity window and Prefect idempotency issues; weak quality per manifest — content present but several sections are thinner than ideal.

frontend.content: MDX for pricing, feature, and testimonial blocks

security.https: Vercel enforced HTTPS with HSTS preload
security.form: CSRF token on sign-up form submission Edge Function

data.crm: HubSpot API for sign-up lead capture

devops.ci: GitHub Actions for lint and build validation

reasoner_reviews.product: The pricing tier table and integration logo grid are the highest-conversion elements for a DevSecOps-audience product. Both must load above the fold on desktop and be accessible without JavaScript for SEO crawlers. The sign-up form CRM integration is the single critical path for lead capture.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Next.js 14 static export with Vercel Edge Network is the correct architecture. The sign-up form submission should use a Vercel Edge Function to forward to HubSpot API server-side, keeping the HubSpot API key out of the client bundle. MDX content files allow marketing to update pricing and testimonials without touching React components.
reasoner_reviews.security: CSP header must block inline scripts. HubSpot API key must be a Vercel environment variable, never exposed in client-side bundles. CSRF token on the form submission Edge Function prevents automated spam submissions from third-party pages.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Lighthouse 95+ requires next/image for WebP conversion, lazy-loaded demo video with poster frame only on initial load, and no render-blocking third-party scripts. Plausible cookieless analytics avoids the conversion loss associated with cookie consent banners.

frontend.app_structure: Next.js 14 with static export (output: export). Pages: index (hero, pricing, integrations, sign-up form), /privacy, /404. MDX content files in /content/pricing.mdx, /content/testimonials.mdx, /content/integrations.mdx loaded at build time via next-mdx-remote.
frontend.pages_and_flows: Single-page scroll layout with anchor navigation: #hero (product tagline, CTA button), #pricing (tier cards from MDX), #integrations (logo grid from MDX), #testimonials (quote carousel), #signup (email form). Demo video section lazy-loaded with poster frame only on initial render.
frontend.performance_notes: next/image for all logo and screenshot assets with automatic WebP conversion. Demo video loaded with poster attribute; autoplay disabled. Tailwind JIT to minimise CSS bundle. Plausible script loaded with async defer. Target: LCP < 1.5s, CLS < 0.05.
frontend.frontend_security_notes: Content-Security-Policy set in next.config.js headers: default-src self; script-src self plausible.io; connect-src self plausible.io /api/csrf /api/signup; img-src self data:. No inline event handlers in JSX. Sign-up form React component fetches CSRF token from GET /api/csrf on mount.

security.auth_design: No authentication on public site. Sign-up form Edge Function validates HMAC-SHA256 CSRF token (generated by GET /api/csrf using Web Crypto subtle.sign) before forwarding to HubSpot API.
security.secrets_management: HUBSPOT_API_KEY and CSRF_SECRET stored as Vercel encrypted environment variables; never included in static export build output or client-side JS bundles.
security.abuse_prevention: Sign-up form Edge Function rate-limited to 10 submissions per IP per hour via Vercel KV counter with 3600-second TTL. Honeypot hidden field catches automated form bots.

devops.deployment_topology: Vercel project connected to GitHub repository. Production deployment from main branch. Automatic preview deployment for every PR. Edge Network serves static assets globally from 40-plus PoPs.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions workflow on PR: ESLint + TypeScript check, next build dry-run, broken-link checker on MDX files. Merge to main triggers Vercel production deployment. Lighthouse CI Action runs against production URL post-deploy as required status check failing if score < 95.
devops.observability_stack: Plausible Analytics tracks page views, sign-up form conversion events (custom goals: Form Started and Form Submitted), and outbound clicks to docs. Vercel build error notifications to Slack channel via webhook integration.

ISS-001.title: Demo video loads eagerly causing LCP regression
ISS-001.detail: Previous plan loaded full video file on page load, causing LCP > 3s on 4G and failing the Lighthouse 95 target.

ISS-002.title: HubSpot API key exposed in client bundle
ISS-002.detail: Round 1 plan used a client-side HubSpot form embed that exposed the portal ID in the browser; round 2 moved submission to an Edge Function.

ISS-003.title: Missing CSRF protection on sign-up form Edge Function
ISS-003.detail: Edge Function validates rate limiting but does not verify a CSRF token, allowing cross-origin form submissions from third-party pages.

focus_issues.title: Missing CSRF protection on sign-up form Edge Function
focus_issues.detail: Edge Function validates rate limiting but does not verify a CSRF token, allowing cross-origin form submissions from third-party pages.

latest_recommendations: Add HMAC-SHA256 CSRF token generation endpoint GET /api/csrf and validate token on POST /api/signup Edge Function using Web Crypto subtle.verify
Wire Lighthouse CI gate as required GitHub Actions check, not just informational report

convergence_state.reason: ISS-003 CSRF gap unresolved

previous_audits.summary: Strong static site architecture with good performance story; one remaining security gap on form CSRF.

Demo video lazy-load fix resolves LCP regression
Plausible cookieless analytics correctly avoids cookie consent requirement
