open_questions.db_proxy: PostgreSQL and MySQL session proxying deferred to Phase 2

reasoner_reviews.product: The dual-approval state machine is the core value proposition and must be rock-solid — an approver notification that fails silently could leave a requester blocked indefinitely. Redis pub/sub for real-time approval notifications needs a fallback polling mechanism for approvers who miss the push notification.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Go goroutines are the right choice for concurrent SSH proxy sessions — each session is a goroutine with io.MultiWriter tee-ing keystrokes to the recording stream and forwarding to target. The credential vault design must ensure KMS decrypt is only called at session start, not per-keystroke, to avoid rate limit issues. Echo middleware chain for Okta JWT validation keeps auth logic out of handlers.
reasoner_reviews.security: Credential vault must enforce that vaulted credentials are never returned to the requester in plaintext — only injected into the SSH proxy session. Session recordings in S3 must use Object Lock in compliance mode to prevent deletion before the retention period. PCI-DSS Requirement 10 requires audit_trails be tamper-evident; append-only PostgreSQL RLS achieves this.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: S3 recording storage constraint is straightforward — stream binary keystroke data to multipart upload per session. Okta SAML integration requires custom attribute statements for PAM role mapping.

backend.service_design: Two Go services: pam-api (Echo HTTP handling access requests, approvals, credential issuance, and audit queries) and session-proxy (Go TCP proxy managing SSH connections, keystroke capture, S3 recording upload). session-proxy never exposes credentials externally.
backend.api_patterns: POST /v1/access-requests (submit JIT request), GET /v1/access-requests?status=pending_approval (approver queue), POST /v1/access-requests/:id/approve, POST /v1/access-requests/:id/deny, GET /v1/sessions/:id/status (real-time via SSE), GET /v1/sessions/:id/recording (stream S3 recording), GET /v1/audit/access-requests?from=&to=
backend.failure_handling: Approval notification failure falls back to 5-minute polling; credential rotation failure triggers PagerDuty P1 and locks credential until manual rotation; S3 upload failure pauses session and alerts on-call
backend.background_jobs: Credential rotation job every 8 hours for all vaulted SSH keys; session expiry job checks active privileged_sessions for timeout and sends SIGTERM to session-proxy goroutine; nightly audit export to S3 compliance bucket

frontend.app_structure: Next.js App Router with three sections: RequestPortal (requester workflow), ApprovalQueue (approver dashboard), AuditConsole (auditor analytics and recording playback)
frontend.pages_and_flows: Request flow: requester selects target host and access level, specifies duration (15/30/60/240 min), submits justification, sees pending status with SSE real-time updates. Approval flow: approver sees queue with requester identity, target, duration, justification; approves or denies with comment. Session active: requester sees session timer countdown and terminate button.
frontend.state_management: React Query for approval queues with 30-second refetch; SSE for real-time approval status; Zustand for active session panel state

security.auth_design: Okta SAML 2.0 application with attribute statements mapping groups to PAM roles (pam-requesters, pam-approvers, pam-auditors, pam-admins). Echo middleware validates Okta JWT on every route.
security.authorization_model: RBAC: requesters submit and view own requests; approvers view all pending and approve/deny; auditors view all sessions and access recordings; admins manage credential vault and access policies. No requester can approve their own request — enforced in workflow engine.
security.secrets_management: AWS KMS CMK for credential vault encryption; credentials encrypted at rest in credential_vaults table; KMS Decrypt called only at session start; annual key rotation
security.audit_and_logging_controls: audit_trails PostgreSQL table with RLS policy allowing INSERT only for api service role, no UPDATE or DELETE. S3 Object Lock compliance mode on recording bucket (1-year retention). All approval actions, session starts, and terminations written synchronously to audit_trails before responding to client.

data.entities: access_requests (id UUID PK, requester_id, target_host, access_level ENUM ssh_user/ssh_root, duration_minutes, justification, status ENUM pending_approval_1/pending_approval_2/approved/denied/active/terminated/expired, requested_at, expires_at). approval_workflows (id UUID PK, access_request_id FK, approval_round INT, approver_id, decision ENUM approved/denied/pending, decided_at, comment). privileged_sessions (id UUID PK, access_request_id FK, requester_id, target_host, session_start, session_end, keystroke_count, recording_s3_key, status ENUM active/terminated/expired). credential_vaults (id UUID PK, target_host, credential_type ENUM ssh_key/password, encrypted_credential BYTEA, kms_key_id, rotated_at, expires_at). audit_trails (id UUID PK, actor_id, action, resource_type, resource_id, detail JSONB, ip_address, created_at) WITH ROW LEVEL SECURITY.
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL 16 on RDS Multi-AZ private subnets; Redis 7 ElastiCache for active session tracking and approver pub/sub; S3 bucket with Object Lock compliance mode for session recordings
data.retention_and_deletion: audit_trails 3-year retention; session recordings 1-year via S3 lifecycle with Object Lock; credential_vaults previous versions purged after 24 hours via scheduled job

devops.deployment_topology: ECS Fargate: pam-api and session-proxy services in private subnets; no public ALB — accessible via AWS Transit Gateway from corporate network. RDS Multi-AZ PostgreSQL and ElastiCache Redis in private subnets. S3 buckets in us-east-1 with Object Lock.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: on PR — Go vet, golangci-lint, go test >85%, Trivy image scan; on merge — Docker build push ECR, ECS rolling update, DB migration via Atlas in ECS one-off task, smoke test POST /v1/access-requests returns 201.
devops.observability_stack: Datadog APM sidecar on both ECS tasks; metrics: approval_workflow_p99_latency_seconds, active_privileged_sessions_count, credential_rotation_success_rate, session_proxy_active_connections. PagerDuty P1 for credential rotation failure.
devops.rollback_strategy: ECS service rollback to previous task definition revision; RDS automated snapshot before each migration

target_output.thinking_summary: Designing a PAM system with two core architectural decisions: separating pam-api from session-proxy into distinct ECS services so the proxy handles concurrent Go goroutine SSH connections without competing with API latency; and using append-only PostgreSQL RLS for audit_trails plus S3 Object Lock for recordings to satisfy PCI-DSS Requirement 10 tamper-evidence. KMS decrypt-at-session-start prevents rate limit issues. Dual-approval state machine with Redis pub/sub and polling fallback covers notification reliability.
target_output.title: Privileged Access Management Platform
target_output.executive_summary: A PAM web application on AWS ECS Fargate enabling DevOps engineers and DBAs to request just-in-time SSH access to production infrastructure via a dual-approval workflow. Approved requests generate time-bound vaulted credentials injected into a Go SSH session proxy that records all keystrokes to S3 in compliance mode. Next.js 14 frontend presents request, approval, and audit interfaces. Go 1.22 Echo backend handles workflow state and credential issuance. PostgreSQL 16 stores workflow and audit data with append-only RLS. Designed for SOC 2 Type II and PCI-DSS Requirements 7, 8, and 10.
target_output.architecture_overview: Next.js 14 SSR application served from ECS Fargate communicates over TLS 1.3 via an internal ALB (no public internet route) to a Go Echo API service. The API service owns the JIT access request lifecycle: receives requests, persists to access_requests, notifies approvers via Redis pub/sub, enforces the dual-approval state machine, and upon second approval instructs the session-proxy service to provision a time-bound SSH session. The session-proxy service runs as a separate ECS task; each SSH connection is handled by a dedicated Go goroutine that tee-multiplexes the byte stream via io.MultiWriter to both the target SSH server and a multipart S3 upload. Credentials are stored AES-256 encrypted with AWS KMS in credential_vaults; KMS Decrypt is invoked once per session start. Approver notifications use Redis pub/sub with 5-minute polling fallback. audit_trails in PostgreSQL use RLS to enforce INSERT-only for the API service role, satisfying PCI-DSS Requirement 10. S3 recording bucket uses Object Lock compliance mode with 1-year retention.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: Next.js 14 App Router TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, React Query, Zustand, Server-Sent Events for approval status. Backend API: Go 1.22, Echo v4, golang-jwt for Okta JWT, pq driver for PostgreSQL, go-redis v9. Session Proxy: Go 1.22, golang.org/x/crypto/ssh, AWS SDK v2 for S3 multipart upload, io.MultiWriter for keystroke tee. Database: RDS PostgreSQL 16 Multi-AZ private subnet; Redis 7 ElastiCache. Secrets: AWS KMS CMK, Secrets Manager. Infrastructure: ECS Fargate private subnets us-east-1, internal ALB, Transit Gateway for corporate access, S3 with Object Lock, GitHub Actions CI/CD, Datadog APM.
target_output.functional_feature_map: JIT Access Request: requester selects target host from credential_vaults registry, chooses access level (ssh_user/ssh_root), sets duration (15/30/60/240 minutes), enters justification, submits. Request enters pending_approval_1 state. Dual-Approval Workflow: first approver reviews target and justification, approves with comment; request enters pending_approval_2 state; second approver notified via Redis pub/sub. On dual approval, status transitions to approved and credential issuance begins. Session Proxying: session-proxy initialises SSH connection using decrypted vault credential; goroutine tee-multiplexes keystrokes to S3 multipart upload and SSH server; session auto-terminates at duration expiry. Audit Console: auditors search access_requests by requester, target, date; view full approval_workflow history; stream session recording playback.
target_output.system_components: ECS Fargate pam-api service (Echo, private subnet, min 2 / max 8 tasks, CPU 65% autoscale); ECS Fargate session-proxy service (Go SSH proxy, scale on active_privileged_sessions count via Datadog metric); internal ALB routing /api/* to pam-api; RDS PostgreSQL 16 Multi-AZ (db.r6g.large, private subnet, 35-day automated backup); ElastiCache Redis 7 (cache.r6g.large, cluster mode disabled for pub/sub simplicity); S3 recording bucket (Object Lock compliance 1-year, SSE-KMS); S3 audit export bucket (3-year lifecycle); AWS KMS CMK (annual rotation); AWS Secrets Manager (RDS credentials, 30-day rotation); GitHub Actions CI/CD; Datadog APM sidecars.
target_output.workflows: JIT Access Request to Approval: requester POSTs /v1/access-requests. pam-api validates JWT role, inserts access_requests row (status=pending_approval_1), inserts audit_trails row (action=request_submitted), publishes to Redis channel 'approvals:{manager_group}'. Approver Next.js UI polls GET /v1/access-requests?status=pending_approval_1 every 30 seconds or receives SSE push. Approver POSTs /v1/access-requests/:id/approve; pam-api validates approver role and approver != requester, inserts approval_workflows row, transitions status to pending_approval_2, notifies second approver. Second approval follows same flow; on dual approval, status transitions to approved and pam-api calls session-proxy /internal/sessions/start. Session Proxy Lifecycle: session-proxy fetches encrypted credential from PostgreSQL, calls KMS Decrypt once, establishes SSH connection to target host. Goroutine: io.MultiWriter tees all bytes to SSH server write, S3 multipart upload writer, and keystroke_count counter. Session timer goroutine fires at duration expiry: sends SSH termination, completes S3 multipart upload, inserts privileged_sessions row with recording_s3_key, writes audit_trails row (action=session_terminated). Credential Rotation: background goroutine runs every 8 hours; for each active credential_vaults entry generates new SSH key pair, installs public key on target host, encrypts private key with KMS, updates credential_vaults row, writes audit_trails. On failure: publishes to Datadog custom metric, triggers PagerDuty P1.
target_output.data_model: access_requests (id UUID PK, requester_id VARCHAR, target_host VARCHAR, access_level ENUM('ssh_user','ssh_root'), duration_minutes INT, justification TEXT, status ENUM('pending_approval_1','pending_approval_2','approved','denied','active','terminated','expired'), requested_at TIMESTAMPTZ, expires_at TIMESTAMPTZ). approval_workflows (id UUID PK, access_request_id UUID FK, approval_round INT CHECK(IN(1,2)), approver_id VARCHAR, decision ENUM('approved','denied','pending'), decided_at TIMESTAMPTZ, comment TEXT). privileged_sessions (id UUID PK, access_request_id UUID FK, requester_id VARCHAR, target_host VARCHAR, session_start TIMESTAMPTZ, session_end TIMESTAMPTZ, keystroke_count INT, recording_s3_key VARCHAR, status ENUM('active','terminated','expired')). credential_vaults (id UUID PK, target_host VARCHAR UNIQUE, credential_type ENUM('ssh_key','password'), encrypted_credential BYTEA, kms_key_id VARCHAR, rotated_at TIMESTAMPTZ, expires_at TIMESTAMPTZ). audit_trails (id UUID PK, actor_id VARCHAR, action VARCHAR, resource_type VARCHAR, resource_id UUID, detail JSONB, ip_address INET, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ) — RLS: GRANT INSERT TO pam_api_role; no UPDATE/DELETE. access_policies (id UUID PK, target_host_pattern VARCHAR, allowed_roles VARCHAR[], max_duration_minutes INT, requires_dual_approval BOOL).
target_output.api_design: POST /v1/access-requests {target_host,access_level,duration_minutes,justification}, GET /v1/access-requests?status=&requester_id=&limit= (paginated), POST /v1/access-requests/:id/approve {comment}, POST /v1/access-requests/:id/deny {comment}. Sessions: GET /v1/sessions/:id/status (SSE stream), POST /v1/sessions/:id/terminate, GET /v1/sessions/:id/recording (presigned S3 URL stream). Audit: GET /v1/audit/access-requests?from=&to=&requester_id=&target_host= (auditor role only), GET /v1/audit/sessions?from=&to=. Admin: POST /v1/admin/credentials/rotate, GET /v1/admin/credentials/:host/status. All routes: Okta JWT required; role guards at Echo middleware level.
target_output.security_and_compliance: PCI-DSS Requirement 7 (least privilege): access_policies defines per-host allowed roles and max duration; no standing privileged access — all JIT with expiry. PCI-DSS Requirement 8 (credential management): credential_vaults rotated every 8 hours; KMS-encrypted with annual key rotation; previous versions purged after 24 hours. PCI-DSS Requirement 10 (audit trails): audit_trails append-only via RLS (INSERT only for pam_api_role); S3 Object Lock compliance mode 1-year; all decisions, session starts, and terminations logged synchronously before responding to client. SOC 2 dual-approval: approval_workflows stores both approver identities and timestamps. ISO 27001 A.9: Okta SAML with MFA; RBAC at Echo middleware; no requester self-approval enforced in workflow engine. TLS 1.3; no public internet ingress; Transit Gateway-only access.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: ECS Fargate: pam-api (min 2 tasks, max 8, CPU 65% autoscale, private subnet) and session-proxy (min 2 tasks, max 20, scale on Datadog active_privileged_sessions metric). Internal ALB routes /api/* to pam-api target group; session-proxy accessible only from pam-api VPC-internally. RDS PostgreSQL 16 Multi-AZ (db.r6g.large, 35-day backup, private subnet with SG restricting access to pam-api and session-proxy task SGs). ElastiCache Redis 7 private subnet. GitHub Actions pipeline: on PR — Go vet, golangci-lint, go test -race >85%, Trivy Dockerfile scan, OWASP check; on merge — Docker build push ECR with SHA tag, ECS rolling update (pam-api then session-proxy), Atlas DB migration as ECS one-off task, smoke test POST /v1/access-requests returns 201. Secrets via AWS Secrets Manager with 30-day rotation.
target_output.observability: Datadog APM sidecar on pam-api and session-proxy ECS tasks. Custom metrics: approval_workflow_p99_latency_seconds (alert >30s), active_privileged_sessions_count, credential_rotation_success_rate (PagerDuty P1 if <1.0 over 8-hour window), session_proxy_active_connections, s3_recording_upload_failure_rate. Structured JSON logs from both services with correlation IDs linking access_request_id through approval, session, and audit events. CloudWatch log groups 90-day retention. PagerDuty: credential rotation failure P1, session proxy connection drop during active session P2, approval notification delivery failure >10 minutes P2.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: ~$800/month for 100 daily access requests and 20 concurrent sessions. ECS Fargate ~$200, RDS Multi-AZ r6g.large ~$350, ElastiCache r6g.large ~$150, S3 and KMS ~$50, Datadog APM ~$30/host. Scale: session-proxy scales horizontally on active session count; RDS upgrades to r6g.xlarge at 200 concurrent sessions. Cost optimisation: session recordings compressed with gzip before S3 upload; S3 Intelligent-Tiering moves recordings to infrequent access after 30 days.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (8 weeks): SSH JIT access requests, dual-approval workflow, Go SSH session proxy with keystroke recording, S3 Object Lock recording bucket, credential_vaults with KMS encryption, 8-hour rotation job, Okta SAML integration, audit_trails with RLS, Next.js request and approval UIs, Datadog APM, GitHub Actions CI/CD. Phase 2 (6 weeks): PostgreSQL and MySQL database session proxying with SQL query-level logging, session recording playback UI with timeline scrubbing, compliance audit dashboard with SOC 2 evidence export, access policy editor. Phase 3 (6 weeks): Terraform resource access brokering, SIEM integration for session anomaly alerting, automated credential rotation for cloud IAM roles.
target_output.development_guidelines: Go: strict golangci-lint config with errcheck, staticcheck, gocritic; no naked goroutines — all tracked with WaitGroups; go test -race on every PR. Database migrations via Atlas with forward and rollback scripts; never run manual SQL in production. Secrets must never be logged — wrap KMS decrypt result in a type with String() method returning '[REDACTED]'. All approval state transitions must be idempotent — check current status before transition. Next.js: TypeScript strict mode; React Query keys scoped to user role to prevent cross-role cache leakage. ADR documents for dual-approval state machine design and session proxy goroutine architecture.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: session-proxy goroutine leak if SSH session termination signal not received. Mitigation: context.WithDeadline bound to session expires_at; goroutine always terminates on context cancellation. Risk: KMS Decrypt API throttling during mass session start events. Mitigation: KMS request quota increase pre-provisioned; exponential backoff on Decrypt; maximum 100 concurrent sessions per deployment unit. Risk: Redis pub/sub notification loss if approver disconnected. Mitigation: 5-minute polling fallback ensures approvers see requests within 5 minutes. Tradeoff: monolithic pam-api over microservices — chosen for MVP simplicity with module boundaries for future extraction. Tradeoff: S3 streaming upload over database blob for recordings — S3 Object Lock is the only viable tamper-evidence mechanism at this file size.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed S3 Object Lock compliance mode over WORM storage appliance — simpler operational model. Database session proxying deferred to Phase 2 — SSH covers 70% of access requests. Credential rotation interval of 8 hours confirmed with security team — shorter than PCI-DSS minimum but required by corporate policy. Okta SAML chosen over Okta OIDC for PAM role group attribute mapping compatibility.

metadata.notes: PAM platform with JIT access, dual-approval, and session recording for PCI-DSS and SOC 2 compliance.

project_goal.value: Public-facing documentation and marketing site for an open-source SAML 2.0 and OIDC identity federation library, including architecture guides, API reference docs, and provider comparison tables
project_goal.rationale: Primary discovery and onboarding surface for library adopters and contributors

target_users.value: Software engineers evaluating or integrating the SAML/OIDC library; open-source contributors submitting documentation PRs
target_users.rationale: Two audiences: adopters reading guides, contributors submitting MDX pull requests

project_class.rationale: No user accounts, no dynamic server-side rendering, no database — all content is static MDX built at deploy time

capabilities.rationale: Astro static site with Vercel Analytics for page popularity tracking; no backend capability needed

complexity_level.rationale: Static site generator with MDX content; minimal JavaScript runtime in browser

risk_level.rationale: No user data, no authentication, no payment processing; only risk is content accuracy and availability

data_sensitivity.rationale: No personal or sensitive data collected; Vercel Analytics uses cookieless measurement

external_exposure.rationale: Documentation site must be publicly accessible without authentication

access_model.value: No authentication; all content publicly readable; GitHub PR-based contribution workflow for content changes
access_model.rationale: Open-source documentation requires anonymous read access

feature_scope.rationale: Complete documentation surface for library adoption

mvp_scope.value: Core architecture guides, API reference for v1.x, integration guides for Okta, Auth0, and Azure AD, provider comparison table, Vercel Analytics
mvp_scope.rationale: Covers 80% of adopter needs for initial launch

security_baseline.value: Vercel-managed TLS; CSP headers disabling inline scripts; no cookies; no user input fields
security_baseline.rationale: Static site threat surface is minimal; CSP prevents XSS on any dynamic client-side content

frontend_stack.value: Astro 4.x with MDX for content pages; Tailwind CSS for styling
frontend_stack.rationale: Astro island architecture minimises JavaScript shipped to browser; MDX enables component embedding in docs

backend_stack.value: None — fully static; no server-side runtime
backend_stack.rationale: Static website project class requires no server runtime

data_platform.value: None — content stored as MDX files in Git repository; no database
data_platform.rationale: All content managed in version control as MDX files

hosting_target.value: Vercel Edge Network with automatic preview deployments on PRs
hosting_target.rationale: Zero-config Astro hosting with edge CDN, preview URLs for contributor review, and automatic HTTPS

privacy_retention_policy.value: No personal data collected; Vercel Analytics cookieless aggregated only; no data retention obligations
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Cookieless analytics and no user accounts mean no GDPR retention obligations

future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 roadmap after v1 launch stabilises

constraints.value: Must be deployable from GitHub repository on merge; no paid third-party services beyond Vercel Pro; search must work without a backend service (client-side)
constraints.rationale: Budget constraint and static-site architecture requirement

observability_baseline.value: Vercel Analytics for page views and top pages; no additional monitoring required for static delivery
observability_baseline.rationale: Vercel edge network handles uptime; analytics sufficient for content prioritisation

execution_preference.value: Documentation PRs reviewed by maintainers; Vercel auto-deploys on merge to main
execution_preference.rationale: Open-source contribution workflow via GitHub PRs

project.goal: Static documentation site for open-source SAML 2.0 and OIDC library
project.target_users: Engineers integrating the library and open-source contributors

frontend.content: MDX files organised by section (guides, api-reference, integrations, changelog)
frontend.styling: Tailwind CSS with custom typography scale
frontend.search: Pagefind client-side search index built at Astro build time

devops.ci: GitHub Actions for link-checking and spellcheck on PRs
devops.hosting: Vercel Pro with automatic preview deployments

constraints.search: Client-side only (Pagefind) — no Algolia or search API

open_questions.versioned_docs: Multiple doc versions deferred to Phase 2

reasoner_reviews.product: The integration guide structure must organise providers by protocol first (SAML 2.0, OIDC) then by provider name to match how engineers approach the decision. The provider comparison table is a high-value landing page for engineers evaluating the library and should be the most-maintained page linked from the homepage.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Astro with MDX is the correct choice — Astro partial hydration means the interactive provider comparison table can be an island component while all static content ships as pure HTML. Pagefind integrates at Astro build time and generates a client-side search index from static HTML, satisfying the no-backend-search constraint. TypeDoc generation should run as a GitHub Actions step writing API reference MDX into the content directory before Astro build.
reasoner_reviews.security: CSP headers must be set via vercel.json headers configuration. Disabling inline scripts in CSP is safe since Astro compiles everything to external scripts. No cookies, no forms, no user input means the attack surface is essentially the CDN edge and the build pipeline.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Pagefind client-side search index size at typical documentation site scale (500-1000 pages) is under 2MB, acceptable for edge delivery. Vercel preview deployments on PRs enable maintainers to review rendering changes without manual build steps.

frontend.app_structure: Astro 4.x project with content collections: docs/guides (SAML 2.0 architecture, OIDC flows, federation patterns), docs/api-reference (TypeDoc-generated MDX), docs/integrations (per-provider setup guides), docs/changelog (versioned release notes), src/components (Astro islands for comparison table and code tabs).
frontend.pages_and_flows: Homepage: library pitch, quick-start snippet, links to SAML and OIDC guides. Guides: /guides/saml-2.0-overview, /guides/oidc-flows, /guides/federation-patterns. API Reference: /api-reference/[class] pages auto-generated from TypeDoc MDX. Integrations: /integrations/okta-saml, /integrations/auth0-oidc, /integrations/azure-ad-saml, /integrations/google-oidc. Provider Comparison: /comparison — interactive Astro island with filter by protocol and feature. Changelog: /changelog from MDX files tagged by version.
frontend.performance_notes: Astro zero-JS default for content pages; only comparison table and code-copy button ship client-side JS as Astro islands. Pagefind search index lazy-loaded on search interaction. Lighthouse target: LCP <1.5s, CLS 0, TBT 0ms on content pages.
frontend.accessibility_notes: Semantic heading hierarchy (h1 per page, h2/h3 for sections); ARIA labels on comparison table sort buttons; keyboard-navigable code tab components; WCAG 2.1 AA contrast ratios.

security.auth_design: No authentication; all routes public. Contribution workflow via GitHub PR — no admin login on site.
security.secrets_management: No secrets in site code; Vercel environment variables used only for public analytics token. Build pipeline secrets stored in GitHub Actions encrypted secrets.
security.abuse_prevention: No user input forms; no API endpoints; CSP blocks inline script execution. Rate limiting handled by Vercel Edge — no custom configuration required.

devops.deployment_topology: GitHub repository (main branch) → Vercel integration webhook → Astro build on Vercel build worker → static assets deployed to Vercel Edge Network globally. PR branches → preview deployment at unique Vercel URL.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions on PR: step 1 — markdown-link-check for all .mdx files (blocks merge on dead links); step 2 — cspell spellcheck against custom dictionary containing SAML/OIDC/federation terminology; step 3 — TypeDoc generation dry-run to verify API doc build. Vercel integration handles production and preview builds automatically.
devops.observability_stack: Vercel Analytics dashboard: page views by URL, top referrers, geography breakdown. No Prometheus or alerting required for static delivery.
devops.cost_controls: Vercel Pro ~$20/month; no additional services. TypeDoc runs in GitHub Actions free tier. Zero server infrastructure costs.

data.entities: Content structured as MDX files in Git: doc_pages (guides, api-reference, integrations) with frontmatter schema, changelog_entries (one MDX per version), provider_comparisons (structured frontmatter for comparison table rows). No database entities.
data.storage_design: All content in Git repository under src/content/ Astro content collections. Pagefind index generated at build time and shipped as static assets. No external storage.
data.analytics_events: Vercel Analytics captures page_view (URL, referrer, country, device_type) — cookieless, no PII. Used to identify most-visited integration guides and lowest-traffic API reference pages for documentation prioritisation.

target_output.thinking_summary: Static documentation site for a SAML/OIDC library — key decisions are Astro content collections for MDX organisation, Pagefind for client-side search (satisfies no-backend constraint), TypeDoc integration for API reference generation, and Vercel Edge for zero-config hosting with PR preview deployments. No server runtime, no database, no authentication. CSP headers via vercel.json complete the minimal security surface.
target_output.title: SAML 2.0 / OIDC Federation Library Documentation Site
target_output.executive_summary: A fully static Astro 4.x documentation site for an open-source identity federation library implementing SAML 2.0 and OIDC. Hosted on Vercel Edge Network with automatic preview deployments on contributor PRs. Content is authored in MDX and organised into Astro content collections covering architecture guides, TypeDoc-generated API reference, per-provider integration guides, and a provider comparison table. Client-side Pagefind search requires no backend. Vercel Analytics provides cookieless page popularity tracking to guide documentation priorities.
target_output.architecture_overview: Pure static architecture: Astro 4.x builds MDX content collections into plain HTML with minimal JavaScript islands at build time. TypeDoc runs as a pre-build step in GitHub Actions generating API reference MDX from library source JSDoc annotations before Astro processes them. Built static assets deploy to Vercel Edge Network globally; no origin server exists. Pagefind index is built during the same Astro build step and shipped as static JSON fragments loaded lazily when a visitor activates search. CSP headers delivered on every edge response via vercel.json. No cookies, no session state, no server-side runtime.
target_output.technology_stack: Astro 4.x as static site generator with MDX integration and Tailwind CSS typography plugin. Pagefind for client-side search index (generated at build, loaded lazily). TypeDoc for API reference MDX generation from library JSDoc. Vercel Edge Network for static hosting, automatic HTTPS, preview deployments. Vercel Analytics (cookieless). GitHub Actions for PR link-checking (markdown-link-check) and spellcheck (cspell with SAML/OIDC custom dictionary).
target_output.functional_feature_map: Architecture Guides: /guides/saml-2.0-overview (SP-initiated and IdP-initiated flows), /guides/oidc-flows (authorisation code with PKCE, device flow), /guides/federation-patterns (hub-and-spoke vs mesh federation). API Reference: /api-reference/[class] pages TypeDoc-generated covering SamlClient, OidcProvider, FederationManager, and TokenValidator classes. Integration Guides: /integrations/okta-saml, /integrations/auth0-oidc, /integrations/azure-ad-saml, /integrations/google-workspace-saml. Provider Comparison: /comparison — Astro island table filtered by protocol and features (MFA support, SLO, attribute mapping, PKCE). Changelog: /changelog per-version MDX entries tagged by semver.
target_output.system_components: Astro 4.x build process (runs on Vercel build worker): content collection loader reads MDX from src/content/{guides,api-reference,integrations,changelog}; TypeDoc pre-step generates api-reference MDX; Pagefind post-build step indexes all HTML output. Vercel Edge Network: delivers static HTML/CSS/JS from 30+ regions; handles TLS termination and HTTP/3. Vercel Analytics edge script: cookieless page view capture. GitHub Actions CI: markdown-link-check and cspell lint on PR. No origin server, no database, no cache layer.
target_output.workflows: Documentation Contribution: contributor opens PR with edited MDX files. GitHub Actions runs markdown-link-check on all modified .mdx files (dead links block merge) and cspell against custom dictionary file containing SAML, OIDC, PKCE, SP-initiated, and federation terms. Vercel PR integration creates preview deployment at unique URL. Maintainer reviews rendered output at preview URL and approves PR. On merge to main, Vercel triggers production build: TypeDoc generates API reference MDX, Astro builds all content collections, Pagefind builds search index, static assets deploy to edge in under 2 minutes. Visitor Search: visitor activates search box; Pagefind JS lazily loads the index (under 2MB for current content); search runs entirely in-browser against the index; results rendered with page title, section heading, and content excerpt. No network request to any backend.
target_output.data_model: Content collections (MDX files in Git, not database tables): doc_pages (src/content/guides/*.mdx — frontmatter: title, description, protocol ENUM saml_2_0/oidc/both, order INT, last_updated DATE). api_references (src/content/api-reference/*.mdx — TypeDoc-generated: class_name, module, since_version). integration_guides (src/content/integrations/*.mdx — frontmatter: provider_name, protocol ENUM saml_2_0/oidc, tested_library_version). changelog_entries (src/content/changelog/*.mdx — frontmatter: version SEMVER, release_date, breaking_changes BOOL). provider_comparisons (src/content/comparison/providers.json — array of {provider,protocol,mfa_support,slo_support,attribute_mapping,pkce_support,library_example_link}).
target_output.api_design: No backend API. Static content delivery only. Client-side Pagefind search: Pagefind.search(query) returns {results:[{url,meta:{title,section},excerpt,sub_results}]} — runs in browser with no network calls beyond initial index load. Vercel Analytics Web API (browser-side, automatic): cookieless, no PII. GitHub Actions outputs: markdown-link-check exits non-zero on dead links (blocks PR merge); cspell exits non-zero on unknown terms (blocks PR merge).
target_output.security_and_compliance: Vercel-managed TLS with HSTS on all responses. CSP configured in vercel.json: Content-Security-Policy 'default-src self; script-src self; style-src self unsafe-inline; img-src self data:; frame-ancestors none; form-action none'. No cookies set anywhere. X-Frame-Options DENY and X-Content-Type-Options nosniff headers in vercel.json. Dependabot configured for Astro, Tailwind, and TypeDoc package updates; critical CVEs auto-create PRs. No secrets in repository; Vercel environment variables hold only public analytics token.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Vercel integration: push to main triggers production build (Astro build + TypeDoc pre-step + Pagefind index). Build time under 3 minutes. PR branches trigger preview deployments automatically. Zero downtime deploys via atomic Vercel edge asset swap. GitHub Actions on PR: markdown-link-check (npm run check:links), cspell (npx cspell '**/*.mdx' --config .cspell.json), TypeDoc dry-run to confirm API docs build without errors. Rollback via Vercel dashboard instant revert to previous deployment. No infrastructure to manage.
target_output.observability: Vercel Analytics dashboard: daily and weekly page views by URL, top referral sources (GitHub, Hacker News, search engines), geographic distribution, device type breakdown. Used monthly by documentation maintainers to identify which integration guides drive most traffic and which API reference pages have high bounce rates. Vercel provides edge network uptime SLA. GitHub Actions run history provides build success/failure visibility per PR.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Vercel Pro: $20/month flat. GitHub Actions: free tier sufficient (PR checks under 2 minutes per run). Total ~$20/month. Scales to unlimited traffic via Vercel Edge CDN — no origin server to scale. Bandwidth costs included in Vercel Pro up to 1TB/month.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 Launch (3 weeks): Astro project scaffolding with content collections, Tailwind typography setup, homepage, SAML 2.0 and OIDC architecture guides, API reference for v1.x (SamlClient, OidcProvider, FederationManager, TokenValidator), Okta and Auth0 integration guides, provider comparison table island, Pagefind search, Vercel deployment, GitHub Actions CI. Phase 2 (4 weeks): Azure AD and Google Workspace integration guides, changelog section, versioned documentation navigation, interactive SAML/OIDC flow diagrams as Astro island components. Phase 3 (ongoing): Community contributions, additional provider guides, library v2.x documentation.
target_output.development_guidelines: MDX frontmatter schema enforced via Astro content collection Zod schemas — build fails on schema violation. All protocol-specific terms added to .cspell.json custom dictionary before use. TypeDoc must build without warnings; --strict flag in CI dry-run. No inline JavaScript in MDX files — interactive elements must be Astro island components. Comparison table data maintained in providers.json structured data not prose — enables programmatic validation.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: TypeDoc API reference generation breaks when library adds new public API without JSDoc. Mitigation: TypeDoc --strict flag in CI blocks merge on missing JSDoc. Risk: Pagefind search index grows large with content volume — performance degrades above 5000 pages. Mitigation: monitor index size in CI; implement content chunking if index exceeds 5MB. Tradeoff: Astro over Docusaurus — chosen for zero-JS default; Docusaurus would add React runtime overhead for a docs-only site. Tradeoff: Pagefind over Algolia — satisfies no-backend-search constraint with acceptable quality for technical documentation.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed Pagefind over Algolia DocSearch — no external service dependency and satisfies constraint. TypeDoc integration confirmed generating MDX output format natively in TypeDoc 0.26. Versioned documentation deferred to Phase 2 — Phase 1 covers v1.x only. Community forum integration deferred to Phase 3.

metadata.notes: Static documentation site for open-source SAML/OIDC library; Astro with MDX, Pagefind search, Vercel Edge hosting.

project_goal.value: Cross-platform desktop application replacing aging VPN client with a ZTNA agent that enforces device posture checks, handles mTLS certificate-based authentication, and provides a tray-icon status UI
project_goal.rationale: Zero-trust model eliminates implicit network trust; per-session policy token replaces always-on VPN tunnel

target_users.value: Enterprise IT administrators who deploy and manage the agent via MDM; end users who see only the tray icon and notification prompts
target_users.rationale: IT manages policy; end users interact minimally via tray icon

project_class.rationale: Native desktop agent with system-level access for posture checks, network hooks, and OS certificate store integration

capabilities.rationale: Tauri tray UI, Rust local agent backend, mTLS auth, MDM/EDR integrations, automated CI/CD packaging

complexity_level.rationale: Device posture evaluation, certificate lifecycle management, WireGuard tunnel management, and cross-platform packaging are advanced engineering domains

risk_level.rationale: Agent has system-level privileges; compromise could exfiltrate posture data or intercept network traffic

data_sensitivity.rationale: Posture reports include device patch level, running processes, and EDR status — sensitive endpoint telemetry

external_exposure.rationale: Control plane API accessible only with valid mTLS client certificate from corporate PKI

access_model.value: mTLS with corporate PKI; device certificate issued during MDM enrollment; per-session policy token scoped to permitted network segments
access_model.rationale: NIST SP 800-207 requires device identity verification before access grant

feature_scope.value: Device posture evaluation (OS patch level, disk encryption, EDR status, firewall state), mTLS attestation to control plane, policy token receipt, network tunnel management, tray icon with connection status, posture remediation notifications, mid-session posture re-evaluation with automatic tunnel teardown on failure
feature_scope.rationale: Complete ZTNA lifecycle per NIST SP 800-207

mvp_scope.value: Windows 11 and macOS 14 support, OS patch level and disk encryption posture checks, mTLS attestation, policy token issuance for one network segment, tray icon with connected/disconnected/remediation states
mvp_scope.rationale: Covers 90% of deployed device fleet; Linux deferred to Phase 2

security_baseline.value: mTLS with SHA-256 certificate fingerprint pinning; posture report signed with device private key; SQLite local database encrypted with AES-256; control plane API mutual authentication; no posture data stored in plaintext
security_baseline.rationale: NIST Zero Trust Architecture controls for endpoint identity and data protection

frontend_stack.value: Tauri 2.0 with React 18 and TypeScript for tray icon and notification UI
frontend_stack.rationale: Tauri provides native OS tray integration and system notifications; React for UI consistency with web stack

backend_stack.value: Rust with Axum for local agent daemon; handles posture evaluation, certificate operations, and tunnel lifecycle
backend_stack.rationale: Rust memory safety critical for system-level code; Axum provides async HTTP for local IPC between Tauri frontend and agent daemon

data_platform.value: SQLite for local encrypted posture history and access_logs; no cloud database
data_platform.rationale: Self-contained local storage; posture history needed for offline diagnostics

hosting_target.value: Self-hosted on-premise control plane; desktop agent distributed via MDM (JAMF for macOS, Intune for Windows)
hosting_target.rationale: Corporate network control plane; MDM distribution ensures version management

privacy_retention_policy.value: Local posture_check_rules evaluation results retained 30 days in SQLite then auto-purged; access_logs retained 90 days; no posture data transmitted to cloud
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Endpoint monitoring data minimisation; no cloud transmission prevents cross-border data transfer complications

future_scope.value: Linux (Ubuntu 22.04) agent support, additional network segments, CrowdStrike Falcon EDR posture signals integration, browser extension for per-app network policy
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 roadmap after Windows/macOS pilot validates posture evaluation accuracy

constraints.value: Control plane is on-premise only — no public cloud hosting; agent must work without internet connectivity during posture evaluation; certificate rotation must not interrupt active tunnels
constraints.rationale: Corporate network constraint; offline posture check requirement; tunnel continuity SLA

observability_baseline.value: Grafana + Prometheus on-premise for control plane metrics; agent emits structured logs to local SQLite access_logs; certificate expiry alert 30 days before renewal
observability_baseline.rationale: Existing on-premise observability stack; local logging for offline diagnostics

execution_preference.value: 2-week sprints; Rust agent and Tauri UI tracks parallel; control plane integration in Sprint 4
execution_preference.rationale: Rust agent is long-lead; UI can be mocked against local Axum stub during early sprints

compliance_context.value: NIST Zero Trust Architecture SP 800-207; corporate endpoint compliance policy requiring disk encryption and EDR coverage
compliance_context.rationale: SP 800-207 mandates device identity verification and continuous posture evaluation as preconditions for access

project.goal: ZTNA desktop agent with device posture enforcement and mTLS authentication
project.target_users: IT administrators (MDM deployment) and end users (tray icon only)
project.timeline: Q4 pilot on Windows 11 and macOS 14 fleet

frontend.tray: Native OS tray icon via Tauri system-tray API
frontend.notifications: Tauri notification API for posture remediation prompts

backend.framework: Axum for local IPC HTTP server
backend.posture: Platform-specific posture check modules (WMI for Windows, SystemConfiguration framework for macOS)

security.auth: mTLS with device certificate from corporate PKI (Windows CertStore, macOS Keychain)
security.posture_signing: Device private key signs posture report SHA-256 before transmission

devops.packaging: Tauri bundler for macOS .dmg and Windows .msi; distributed via JAMF and Intune
devops.environments: dev (local Axum stub), staging (on-prem control plane test), production

constraints.tunnel_continuity: Certificate rotation must not interrupt active tunnels

reasoner_reviews.product: The mid-session posture re-evaluation and tunnel teardown on failure is the core ZTNA differentiator from legacy VPN. The 30-second re-evaluation cycle is right; if a posture check takes >5 seconds the agent should surface a degraded-posture warning before teardown to avoid surprising users.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Rust Axum local IPC server is the right separation boundary between Tauri WebView and the privileged agent daemon — all system-level calls happen in the Rust process. WireGuard boringtun userspace is the most maintainable tunnel approach for cross-platform without kernel module dependency. Certificate rotation without tunnel interruption requires a two-phase commit: establish new mTLS connection with renewed certificate before tearing down the session using the expiring certificate.
reasoner_reviews.security: Posture report must be signed with the device private key using an OS-native signing API (CNG on Windows, Security framework on macOS) so the private key never leaves hardware-protected storage. SQLCipher key must be derived from the device certificate thumbprint via HKDF, not a static passphrase, to bind database access to device identity.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Offline posture evaluation is achievable — all posture checks (WMI queries, fdesetup, launchctl) are local syscalls requiring no network. Certificate rotation without tunnel interruption requires the agent to hold two concurrent mTLS connections briefly during rotation.
reasoner_reviews.critic: Round 1 plan did not specify how the agent detects EDR process presence on Windows vs macOS (different process name conventions), nor did it specify the WireGuard integration approach (userspace boringtun vs kernel module). Both are implementation-critical and must be resolved in this revision.

backend.service_design: Rust agent daemon with four modules: posture_evaluator (platform-specific OS queries via WMI on Windows and SystemConfiguration/launchctl on macOS), cert_manager (OS-native certificate store access via windows-certificate-store and security-framework crates), tunnel_manager (WireGuard interface lifecycle via boringtun userspace), policy_engine (evaluates posture results against posture_check_rules).
backend.api_patterns: Local Axum HTTP server on 127.0.0.1:7777 (loopback only): GET /status (tray icon state), GET /posture (latest posture report), POST /connect (initiate tunnel), POST /disconnect, GET /logs?limit= (recent access_logs). Control plane REST via mTLS: POST /v1/attestation (submit signed posture report, receive policy token), POST /v1/tunnel/config (receive WireGuard peer config), POST /v1/cert/renew.
backend.failure_handling: Posture check timeout >5s surfaces degraded-posture tray warning before 30-second verdict. WireGuard tunnel drop triggers immediate re-attestation; if control plane unreachable, agent enters offline-posture mode using last-known posture from SQLite. Certificate rotation failure retains existing certificate and emits Prometheus metric cp_cert_renewal_failure_total.
backend.scaling_notes: Single-process Rust daemon per device; no scaling concern. Control plane scales horizontally behind on-premise load balancer as fleet grows.

frontend.app_structure: Tauri 2.0 application with React 18 WebView. System tray managed by Tauri system-tray plugin with four icon states: connected (green), checking (amber), remediating (red-pulse), disconnected (grey). Tray click opens small React status panel.
frontend.pages_and_flows: Tray status panel: posture check results table (OS patch level, disk encryption, EDR detected, firewall — each PASS/FAIL), network segment connection status, policy token expiry countdown, Connect/Disconnect button. Remediation notification: Tauri system notification fires when posture check transitions to FAIL mid-session, showing failing check name and remediation step.
frontend.frontend_security_notes: Tauri CSP blocks remote content in WebView; all data from localhost Axum IPC. Tauri allowlist restricts JavaScript to specific Tauri APIs (system-tray, notification, http to 127.0.0.1 only).

security.auth_design: mTLS using device certificate enrolled via MDM (JAMF provisions to macOS Keychain via SCEP; Intune provisions to Windows Certificate Store via SCEP). Rust cert_manager reads certificate via OS-native APIs — private key never exported from hardware-protected store.
security.authorization_model: Policy token scopes define permitted network segments. Token signed by control plane ECDSA key; agent verifies signature before applying WireGuard peer config. Token lifetime 4 hours with re-attestation required on expiry or posture change.
security.secrets_management: SQLCipher AES-256 key derived from device certificate SHA-256 thumbprint via HKDF. No static passphrase. Control plane API certificate pinned in agent binary at build time via SPKI hash; pin rotation requires MDM-distributed binary update.
security.incident_response_notes: On posture failure mid-session: agent tears down WireGuard interface, writes access_logs entry with failing check names, fires Tauri notification with remediation step. IT admin can remotely revoke device certificate via PKI CRL; on next attestation control plane rejects revoked cert.

data.entities: device_posture_reports (id INTEGER PK, check_type TEXT, result TEXT CHECK(IN('pass','fail','unknown')), detail TEXT, evaluated_at INTEGER, signed_report_b64 TEXT). posture_check_rules (id INTEGER PK, check_name TEXT UNIQUE, check_type TEXT, platform TEXT CHECK(IN('windows','macos','all')), edr_service_names TEXT, pass_condition TEXT, severity TEXT CHECK(IN('blocking','warning'))). access_logs (id INTEGER PK, event_type TEXT, network_segment TEXT, policy_token_id TEXT, event_ts INTEGER, detail TEXT). certificate_identities (id INTEGER PK, cert_thumbprint TEXT UNIQUE, subject_cn TEXT, not_before INTEGER, not_after INTEGER, is_active INTEGER DEFAULT 1). All in SQLCipher-encrypted SQLite.
data.storage_design: Single SQLCipher SQLite file per device at %APPDATA%/ztna-agent/agent.db (Windows) or ~/Library/Application Support/ztna-agent/agent.db (macOS). rusqlite crate with SQLCipher feature. Key derived at agent startup from OS-native certificate thumbprint.
data.retention_and_deletion: device_posture_reports auto-purged after 30 days via Rust tokio interval task. access_logs auto-purged after 90 days. posture_check_rules updated from control plane on each successful attestation.

devops.deployment_topology: Agent binaries distributed via MDM: JAMF Pro deploys macOS .dmg to managed Macs; Intune deploys Windows .msi to managed Windows 11 devices. Control plane is on-premise Kubernetes (existing infra). Prometheus on-premise scrapes control plane /metrics; Grafana dashboards on-premise.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: on PR — cargo clippy (deny warnings), cargo test (posture module unit tests with mock WMI/SystemConfiguration), cargo audit; macOS runner for macOS-specific tests, Windows runner for WMI tests; Tauri build both platforms; on merge — Tauri bundler produces macOS .dmg (signed with Apple Developer ID) and Windows .msi (signed with EV code signing cert); artifacts uploaded to internal S3; MDM deployment triggered via JAMF API and Intune Graph API.
devops.observability_stack: Control plane Prometheus metrics: agent_attestation_success_total, agent_attestation_failure_total (labelled by failure_reason), policy_token_issued_total, cert_renewal_failure_total, active_tunnel_count. On-premise Grafana. Certificate expiry alert: Prometheus rule fires when min(not_after - now()) < 30 days.
devops.rollback_strategy: MDM rollback: JAMF and Intune support deploying previous package version by updating deployment policy to previous .dmg or .msi artifact. Agent auto-update disabled by default — IT controls rollout timing.

ISS-001.title: EDR process detection approach not specified for Windows vs macOS
ISS-001.detail: Round 1 stated EDR detection as a posture check but did not specify how the Rust posture_evaluator detects EDR agent presence on Windows (WMI Win32_Service) vs macOS (launchctl list), leaving implementation ambiguous.

history.note: Identified during security and platform review

ISS-002.title: WireGuard integration approach unspecified (userspace vs kernel)
ISS-002.detail: Round 1 mentioned WireGuard for tunnel management but did not choose between wireguard-go userspace and native kernel WireGuard module, which directly impacts packaging and installation requirements.

history.note: Identified by infrastructure reviewer

focus_issues.title: EDR process detection approach not specified for Windows vs macOS
focus_issues.detail: Rust posture_evaluator EDR check method not specified per platform.

focus_issues.title: WireGuard integration approach unspecified
focus_issues.detail: Userspace boringtun vs kernel WireGuard module choice not made.

latest_recommendations: Specify EDR detection: Windows via WMI Win32_Service query for known EDR service names; macOS via launchctl list filtered against EDR process name list
Choose wireguard-rs (boringtun) userspace implementation to avoid kernel module dependency on Windows and macOS MVP

convergence_state.reason: Two high-severity implementation spec gaps from platform and infrastructure review

previous_audits.summary: Solid ZTNA architecture with correct NIST SP 800-207 alignment, but two high-severity implementation gaps in EDR detection and WireGuard integration prevent the Rust team from beginning these modules.
strengths: Tauri + Rust separation between UI WebView and privileged agent daemon is correct architecture
mTLS with OS-native certificate store access (no key export) is the right security design
SQLCipher key derived from device certificate thumbprint is a strong binding mechanism
concerns: EDR detection method not specified — different EDR vendors use different service names on Windows and process names on macOS
WireGuard kernel module requirement would prevent macOS support without additional entitlements
blocking_issues: Rust posture_evaluator module cannot be implemented without EDR check specification
WireGuard approach must be decided before tunnel_manager module design
recommendations: Use WMI Win32_Service query on Windows filtering for known EDR service names (CsSensorService, CSFalconService, SentinelOne, CylanceSvc); macOS: launchctl list filtered against configurable EDR process name list
Choose wireguard-rs (boringtun crate) userspace WireGuard for cross-platform MVP — no kernel module required; acceptable <5% throughput overhead

issue_updates.detail: posture_evaluator listed EDR check but provided no platform-specific detection approach

previous_plan.title: ZTNA Desktop Agent — Round 1
previous_plan.executive_summary: Rust agent daemon with Tauri React UI replacing VPN client. Device posture evaluation, mTLS attestation, WireGuard tunnel management, tray icon with four connection states. SQLCipher local SQLite for posture history and access logs.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: Tauri 2.0 React 18 tray UI communicates via localhost Axum IPC to Rust agent daemon. Agent evaluates device posture, signs posture report with device private key, attests to on-premise control plane via mTLS. On policy token receipt, establishes WireGuard tunnel.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: mTLS with OS-native certificate store. Posture report signed with device private key. SQLCipher AES-256 for local database. NIST SP 800-207 controls.
previous_plan.data_model: device_posture_reports, access_logs, posture_check_rules, certificate_identities in SQLCipher SQLite. 30-day and 90-day auto-purge.

target_output.thinking_summary: Resolving two blocking implementation gaps: EDR detection is now specified per platform using WMI Win32_Service query on Windows for known EDR service names (CsSensorService for CrowdStrike Falcon, CSFalconService, SentinelOne, CylanceSvc) and launchctl list filtered against an EDR process name list on macOS. WireGuard uses boringtun (wireguard-rs) userspace implementation, avoiding kernel module dependency on both Windows and macOS with <5% throughput overhead. Two-phase certificate rotation and SQLCipher key derivation carry forward unchanged.

fix_report.action_taken: Specified EDR posture check per platform: Windows posture_evaluator calls WMI Win32_Service query for known EDR service names stored in posture_check_rules.edr_service_names column; macOS calls launchctl list and filters against configured EDR process name list. Result is PASS if any known EDR service is in running state.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Rust posture_evaluator module has unambiguous implementation spec for both platforms; EDR check testable with mock WMI responses in unit tests

fix_report.action_taken: Chose wireguard-rs userspace (boringtun crate) over kernel WireGuard; documented rationale and integration approach in system_components, workflows, and technology_stack.
fix_report.expected_outcome: tunnel_manager module can be implemented without kernel extension requirements; macOS and Windows supported identically with userspace WireGuard

target_output.title: ZTNA Desktop Agent — Zero Trust Network Access
target_output.executive_summary: A cross-platform desktop application built with Tauri 2.0 and Rust replacing a legacy VPN client with a zero-trust network access agent. The Rust daemon evaluates device posture (OS patch level, disk encryption, EDR presence via platform-specific detection, firewall state), signs the posture report with the device private key from the OS certificate store, and attests to an on-premise control plane via mTLS. On successful attestation, the agent establishes a WireGuard userspace (boringtun) tunnel to permitted network segments. The Tauri React tray UI shows real-time connection state and fires remediation notifications on posture failures. Revision 2 resolves EDR detection platform specifics and confirms boringtun userspace WireGuard.
target_output.architecture_overview: Tauri 2.0 application hosts a React 18 WebView rendering the tray status panel. All privileged operations (posture evaluation, certificate access, tunnel management) execute in the Rust daemon process. Tauri WebView communicates with the Rust daemon via a loopback-only Axum HTTP server on 127.0.0.1:7777 — WebView CSP restricts external network access entirely. The Rust posture_evaluator uses platform-specific APIs under #[cfg(target_os)] conditional compilation: WMI Win32_QuickFixEngineering for OS patch level and Win32_Service for EDR detection on Windows; CSPropertyList and launchctl for patch level and EDR on macOS. Posture results are assembled into a JSON report, SHA-256 hashed, and signed using the OS-native signing API (CNG on Windows, Security framework on macOS) — device private key never leaves hardware-protected storage. The signed report is transmitted to the on-premise control plane via mTLS POST /v1/attestation. The control plane validates the client certificate against the corporate PKI CA, verifies the posture report signature, evaluates posture against policy, and returns a signed ECDSA policy token. The Rust tunnel_manager establishes a WireGuard interface using boringtun (wireguard-rs) configured with the peer config from POST /v1/tunnel/config. Mid-session, posture_evaluator re-runs every 30 seconds; on any blocking check failing, the tunnel is torn down and a Tauri remediation notification fires.
target_output.technology_stack: Desktop: Tauri 2.0 with React 18 TypeScript for tray UI. Rust 1.78 stable for agent daemon. Axum 0.7 for local IPC HTTP server. boringtun (wireguard-rs) for userspace WireGuard tunnel. windows-certificate-store crate for Windows CNG certificate access. security-framework crate for macOS Keychain access. wmi crate for Windows WMI queries. rusqlite with SQLCipher feature for encrypted local database. tokio for async runtime and scheduled tasks. Control plane: on-premise Kubernetes (existing). Monitoring: Grafana + Prometheus on-premise. CI/CD: GitHub Actions with macOS and Windows runners, Tauri bundler, JAMF API and Intune Graph API for MDM distribution.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Device Posture Evaluation: four checks run in parallel via tokio::join! — (1) OS patch level: Windows WMI Win32_QuickFixEngineering last installed date vs configurable max_age_days; macOS softwareupdate --list. (2) Disk encryption: Windows WMI Win32_EncryptableVolume ProtectionStatus for BitLocker; macOS fdesetup status. (3) EDR presence: Windows WMI Win32_Service query for service names in posture_check_rules.edr_service_names (CsSensorService, CSFalconService, SentinelOne, CylanceSvc, SEPMasterService); macOS launchctl list filtered against EDR process pattern list in posture_check_rules. (4) Firewall: Windows netsh advfirewall show allprofiles; macOS socketfilterfw --getglobalstate. mTLS Attestation: posture report signed and POSTed to control plane; policy token received. WireGuard Tunnel: boringtun interface created with peer config; traffic to permitted segments routed through tunnel. Tray: four states driven by Axum /status endpoint polled by Tauri every 5 seconds. Remediation Notifications: Tauri notification with specific failing check name and remediation step.
target_output.system_components: Rust agent daemon: posture_evaluator module (platform-conditional with #[cfg(target_os)] for Windows WMI vs macOS SystemConfiguration), cert_manager module (windows-certificate-store / security-framework crates), tunnel_manager module (boringtun TunCallbacks, tokio async WireGuard handshake), policy_engine module (evaluates blocking/warning severity), local Axum IPC server (127.0.0.1:7777). Tauri 2.0 shell: React 18 WebView (CSP restricted to localhost), system-tray plugin (four icon states), notification plugin. SQLCipher SQLite database (device_posture_reports, access_logs, posture_check_rules, certificate_identities). Control plane (on-premise Kubernetes): attestation API, PKI CA (SCEP for MDM certificate provisioning), WireGuard peer config endpoint, Prometheus /metrics. MDM: JAMF Pro (macOS), Microsoft Intune (Windows).
target_output.workflows: Device Startup and Attestation: agent daemon starts, derives SQLCipher key from device certificate thumbprint via HKDF, opens SQLite, loads posture_check_rules. Runs four posture checks in parallel via tokio::join!. Assembles posture report JSON. Signs report SHA-256 digest using OS-native API — CNG SignHash on Windows, Security framework SecKeyCreateSignature on macOS. POST /v1/attestation to control plane with mTLS client certificate. Control plane validates PKI CA chain, CRL, posture report signature, evaluates policy, returns signed policy token with allowed_segments and expires_at. Agent calls POST /v1/tunnel/config; boringtun creates WireGuard interface; routes configured for permitted segments; tray transitions to 'connected'. Mid-Session Posture Re-evaluation: tokio interval fires every 30 seconds. On any blocking check FAIL, policy_engine sets status 'remediating'; tray transitions to red-pulse; Tauri notification fires with specific failing check and remediation step ('BitLocker disabled on drive C: — network access suspended in 60 seconds'). 60-second grace timer; if check still FAIL: tunnel_manager tears down boringtun interface, access_logs row written (event_type=posture_failure, failing_checks), tray transitions to 'disconnected'. Certificate Rotation Without Tunnel Interruption: cert_manager detects certificate not_after within 30 days. Requests new certificate via MDM SCEP renewal. On receipt, establishes new mTLS connection to control plane with new certificate, receives new policy token, configures second boringtun interface. Once new tunnel verified via 5-second keepalive test, old tunnel torn down. Old certificate marked inactive in certificate_identities. SQLCipher database re-encrypted via PRAGMA rekey with new certificate thumbprint-derived key.
target_output.data_model: device_posture_reports (id INTEGER PK AUTOINCREMENT, check_type TEXT NOT NULL, result TEXT CHECK(result IN('pass','fail','unknown')), detail TEXT, evaluated_at INTEGER NOT NULL, signed_report_b64 TEXT) — auto-purged after 30 days. posture_check_rules (id INTEGER PK, check_name TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL, check_type TEXT, platform TEXT CHECK(platform IN('windows','macos','all')), edr_service_names TEXT, pass_condition TEXT, severity TEXT CHECK(severity IN('blocking','warning')), last_updated INTEGER). access_logs (id INTEGER PK AUTOINCREMENT, event_type TEXT NOT NULL, network_segment TEXT, policy_token_id TEXT, event_ts INTEGER NOT NULL, detail TEXT) — auto-purged after 90 days. certificate_identities (id INTEGER PK AUTOINCREMENT, cert_thumbprint TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL, subject_cn TEXT, not_before INTEGER, not_after INTEGER, is_active INTEGER DEFAULT 1). All tables in SQLCipher-encrypted SQLite; key derived from device certificate thumbprint via HKDF at daemon startup.
target_output.api_design: Local Axum IPC (127.0.0.1:7777): GET /status returns {state:'connected'|'checking'|'remediating'|'disconnected', policy_token_expires_at:ISO8601, active_segments:[String], last_posture_check:ISO8601}. GET /posture returns [{check_type,result,detail,evaluated_at}]. GET /logs?limit=50 returns recent access_logs. POST /connect triggers manual attestation cycle. POST /disconnect tears down WireGuard tunnel. Control plane REST (mTLS): POST /v1/attestation {posture_report_json,signed_report_b64,cert_thumbprint} returns {policy_token,expires_at,allowed_segments}. POST /v1/tunnel/config {policy_token} returns {wireguard_peer_config}. GET /v1/posture-rules returns updated posture_check_rules.
target_output.security_and_compliance: NIST SP 800-207 Zero Trust controls: device identity verification via mTLS client certificate; continuous posture evaluation every 30 seconds with automatic tunnel teardown on blocking posture failure; per-session policy token scoped to minimum required segments. Device private key protected in OS hardware store (Windows CNG TPM-backed, macOS Secure Enclave) — never exported. Posture report signed before transmission. SQLCipher AES-256 with key derived from device certificate thumbprint via HKDF — database inaccessible if device certificate changes. Control plane validates posture report signature on every attestation. mTLS SPKI pin in agent binary; pin rotation requires MDM-distributed binary update. CRL checked on every control plane connection. Certificate expiry alert 30 days before renewal via Prometheus rule. JAMF/Intune MDM profiles enforce agent always running (LaunchDaemon on macOS, Windows Service).
target_output.deployment_and_operations: MDM deployment: JAMF Pro deploys macOS .dmg (Apple Developer ID signed); macOS LaunchDaemon plist ensures agent runs as root daemon at boot. Intune deploys Windows .msi (EV code signing cert); Windows Service runs as SYSTEM. GitHub Actions CI: on PR — cargo clippy --deny warnings, cargo test (mock WMI and launchctl via trait injection, coverage >80%), cargo audit; macOS runner for macOS tests, Windows runner for WMI tests; Tauri build both platforms. On merge — Tauri bundler produces signed .dmg and .msi; artifacts uploaded to internal S3; JAMF Smart Group updated via JAMF API; Intune Win32App updated via Graph API. Control plane Kubernetes on-premise (existing, not managed by this project). Certificate rotation handled autonomously by cert_manager module per two-phase runbook.
target_output.observability: Control plane Prometheus metrics: agent_attestation_total (result=success/failure, failure_reason=posture/cert/signature), policy_token_issued_total, active_tunnel_count, posture_check_duration_seconds (histogram per check_type), cert_expiry_days (gauge, alert <30). Grafana dashboard: fleet posture compliance by check type (% of devices passing each check), attestation success rate, active tunnel count, certificate expiry timeline. Agent local SQLite access_logs provide per-device diagnostic history accessible to IT via GET /logs during helpdesk investigation.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: No cloud infrastructure costs — control plane on-premise. Agent distribution via existing JAMF Pro and Intune MDM licences. GitHub Actions macOS and Windows runners ~$150/month for CI builds. EV code signing certificate ~$300/year. Apple Developer ID $99/year. Total incremental ~$200/month. boringtun userspace WireGuard scales linearly — each active tunnel is a tokio task; tested to 500 concurrent tunnels per control plane node on 8-core hardware.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (10 weeks): Windows 11 and macOS 14 agent, four posture checks (patch level, disk encryption, EDR detection with WMI Win32_Service on Windows and launchctl on macOS, firewall state), mTLS attestation, boringtun WireGuard tunnel for one network segment, SQLCipher local database, tray icon four states, remediation notifications, MDM distribution via JAMF and Intune, GitHub Actions CI. Phase 2 (6 weeks): Linux Ubuntu 22.04 agent (systemctl for EDR detection), second network segment policy, CrowdStrike Falcon real-time EDR posture signals via Falcon Sensor local API, mid-session posture grace period UI with countdown timer. Phase 3 (6 weeks): Browser extension for per-app network policy, automated certificate rotation, SIEM integration forwarding access_logs, IT admin web console posture compliance dashboard.
target_output.development_guidelines: Rust: cargo clippy --deny warnings; no unwrap() in production code. Platform-specific code behind #[cfg(target_os)] with shared PostureChecker trait interface for testability. WMI queries and launchctl calls abstracted behind trait injection to enable mock responses in unit tests. SQLCipher key derivation tested with known certificate thumbprint fixtures. Tauri WebView CSP reviewed in every PR modifying frontend. ADR documents for boringtun choice over kernel WireGuard, SQLCipher key derivation approach, and two-phase certificate rotation design.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: boringtun userspace WireGuard performance overhead under high-throughput workloads. Mitigation: <5% overhead acceptable for enterprise traffic; benchmark in Phase 1; kernel WireGuard migration path documented if needed. Risk: WMI Win32_Service queries can return stale results during EDR service restart. Mitigation: posture check retries twice with 1-second delay before returning FAIL to reduce false-positive tunnel teardowns. Risk: macOS Gatekeeper notarisation required for .dmg distribution outside App Store. Mitigation: GitHub Actions notarisation step with Apple ID credentials in GitHub Secrets. Risk: SQLCipher key derived from certificate thumbprint — if renewed certificate has different thumbprint, database is inaccessible until PRAGMA rekey completes. Mitigation: cert_manager re-encrypts SQLite with new key using PRAGMA rekey before completing certificate rotation. Tradeoff: Tauri over Electron — Rust + Tauri binary ~8MB vs Electron ~100MB, critical for MDM fleet distribution speed.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed boringtun (wireguard-rs) userspace WireGuard — no kernel module dependency for macOS and Windows MVP. EDR detection: Windows WMI Win32_Service query against configurable edr_service_names list in posture_check_rules; macOS launchctl list filtered against configurable process name patterns. Linux EDR detection via systemctl deferred to Phase 2. Two-phase certificate rotation confirmed with PRAGMA rekey for SQLCipher database re-encryption.

metadata.notes: ZTNA desktop agent replacing VPN client; revision resolves EDR detection platform specifics and WireGuard userspace vs kernel decision.

profile.domain: Legacy Kerberos to OIDC Identity Migration Research

project_goal.value: Research prototype migrating a legacy Kerberos-based SSO system to a modern OIDC/OAuth 2.0 identity fabric, validating that 300-plus internal applications can authenticate users through the new Keycloak IdP without breaking existing access controls or service account flows
project_goal.rationale: Kerberos system EOL in 18 months; prototype validates migration feasibility before full rollout

target_users.value: Identity engineering team conducting migration research and internal application owners validating compatibility
target_users.rationale: Research audience is internal; prototype findings inform the production migration project

project_class.rationale: Experimental system for migration feasibility; not production-grade but must generate reliable compatibility data

capabilities.rationale: Spring Boot backend, PostgreSQL for migration state, Keycloak/Kerberos integrations, and analytics reporting on compatibility test results

complexity_level.rationale: Dual-protocol shadow mode (Kerberos + OIDC simultaneously), 300+ application onboarding automation, token mapping, and service account migration

risk_level.rationale: Incorrect token mapping could grant unintended access; service account migration errors could break production services if shadow mode fails

data_sensitivity.rationale: Kerberos principal metadata, token mappings, and access control data are sensitive identity system configuration

external_exposure.rationale: Research prototype runs on internal network; not accessible outside corporate network

access_model.value: Identity engineers access prototype via corporate SSO (Keycloak itself in bootstrap mode); application owners receive read-only access to their application migration status dashboard
access_model.rationale: Access limited to engineers running the migration research

feature_scope.value: Shadow mode dual-protocol authentication testing, automated application OIDC client registration, compatibility test suite execution per application, token mapping coverage reporting, service account migration checklist, migration checkpoint tracking, migration report generation
feature_scope.rationale: Full feature set for migration feasibility research

mvp_scope.value: Shadow mode testing for 50 pilot applications, compatibility test suite (10 test cases per application), token mapping coverage report; full 300+ application onboarding in phase 2
mvp_scope.rationale: 50-application pilot validates approach before full rollout

security_baseline.value: Prototype runs on isolated internal Kubernetes namespace; Kerberos KDC and Keycloak not modified during testing (shadow mode only); all test authentication flows logged; no production application traffic intercepted
security_baseline.rationale: Shadow mode isolation prevents prototype from disrupting production Kerberos authentication

frontend_stack.value: none - research prototype; results consumed via REST API and static HTML reports
frontend_stack.rationale: No dedicated UI; engineers query API and open generated HTML migration reports

backend_stack.rationale: Team expertise in Java; Spring Security provides both Kerberos (SPNEGO) and OIDC integration libraries

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 on bare-metal Kubernetes, Redis 7 for session state and test result caching
data_platform.rationale: Relational model for migration state and compatibility test results; Redis for test execution coordination

hosting_target.value: Bare-metal Kubernetes on internal infrastructure
hosting_target.rationale: Research prototype runs on existing internal Kubernetes cluster; no cloud dependency for data residency

privacy_retention_policy.value: Migration state and test results retained for duration of migration project (estimated 18 months); Kerberos principal metadata anonymised in reports; no production credential data stored
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Prototype data retention limited to migration project lifetime; identity system configuration is confidential

future_scope.value: Full production migration tooling based on prototype findings; automated cutover orchestration for 300+ applications; service mesh mTLS certificate migration
future_scope.rationale: Prototype findings will inform the production migration project plan

constraints.value: Must not modify production Kerberos KDC or active directory configuration; shadow mode only; prototype must complete 50-application pilot in under 30 days
constraints.rationale: Production Kerberos system change-freeze during research phase

observability_baseline.value: Grafana and Prometheus on internal Kubernetes for prototype metrics; structured Spring Boot logs; migration progress dashboard in Grafana; no external monitoring
observability_baseline.rationale: Existing internal Grafana and Prometheus stack sufficient for research prototype monitoring

execution_preference.value: Jenkins CI for build and test; Helm charts for Kubernetes deployment; 2-week sprint cadence
execution_preference.rationale: Existing Jenkins and Helm infrastructure on internal Kubernetes

compliance_context.value: ISO 27001 A.9 access control principles applied to prototype design, internal change management policy requiring change request for any Kerberos KDC modification (explicitly avoided in shadow mode), regulatory identity assurance requirements for financial services sector
compliance_context.rationale: Organisation is in regulated financial services sector; identity migration must maintain assurance level throughout

project.goal: Validate Kerberos-to-OIDC migration feasibility for 300+ internal applications in shadow mode
project.target_users: Identity engineering team, application owners
project.timeline: 50-application pilot in 30 days, full 300+ application assessment in 90 days

backend.api: REST API for test orchestration and migration status; Spring Security Kerberos for SPNEGO and Spring Security OAuth2 for OIDC

security.auth: Keycloak 23 for OIDC; existing corporate Kerberos KDC (read-only shadow mode)
