F5 — OpenTelemetry instrumentation: child spans with channel, outcome, and duration_ms attributes emitted on every challenge, verify, and fallback call under the consuming app's active trace context.
target_output.system_components: MfaClient (src/index.ts): public API class; owns pending_challenges Map and FallbackPolicy enforcement; instantiated with MfaClientConfig. TotpChannel (src/channels/totp.ts): RFC 6238 implementation; uses conditional import for SubtleCrypto vs Node.js crypto to support both environments without bundler conditionals. WebAuthnChannel (src/channels/webauthn.ts): thin wrapper around @simplewebauthn/server; dynamically imported to ensure tree-shakeability for TOTP-only bundles. SmsChannel (src/channels/sms.ts): OTP generation and ChallengeState management; delegates to SmsAdapter interface for delivery. Adapter interfaces (src/adapters/): TotpStore, WebAuthnStore, SmsAdapter TypeScript interfaces with JSDoc examples of Redis-backed and in-memory implementations. OtelInstrumentation (src/telemetry.ts): wraps @opentelemetry/api.trace.getActiveSpan() and starts child spans; no-ops gracefully if consuming app has no tracer configured.
target_output.workflows: TOTP Verification Workflow: consuming app calls mfaClient.challenge(userId, 'totp'); SDK creates PendingChallenge with challengeId (UUID v4), stores via TotpStore.storePendingChallenge, returns ChallengeResult with challengeId and expiry. User submits 6-digit code; consuming app calls mfaClient.verify(userId, 'totp', code, challengeId); SDK retrieves pending challenge, checks expiry (5-minute window), retrieves TOTP secret via TotpStore.getSecret, computes HMAC-SHA1 for current and ±1 time windows, compares with timingSafeEqual, calls TotpStore.consumeChallenge on success to prevent replay; returns VerifyResult with outcome and OTel span. WebAuthn Registration Workflow: consuming app calls mfaClient.registerWebAuthn(userId) which calls @simplewebauthn/server.generateRegistrationOptions with rpId and rpName from MfaClientConfig; options returned to browser client; browser calls navigator.credentials.create(); consuming app forwards response to mfaClient.verifyRegistration(userId, response); SDK calls verifyRegistrationResponse with attestation format from config (default 'direct'); on success, credential stored via WebAuthnStore.storeCredential. Fallback Workflow: consuming app calls mfaClient.fallback(userId, {preferredOrder: ['webauthn', 'totp', 'sms']}); SDK attempts challenge on each channel in order until one succeeds, returning the successful ChallengeResult; fallback events emitted as OTel spans with channel and reason attributes.
target_output.data_model: In-SDK in-memory state (not persisted by SDK): pending_challenges: Map<string, PendingChallenge> where PendingChallenge = {challengeId: string (UUID v4), userId: string, channel: 'totp' | 'webauthn' | 'sms', createdAt: number (Unix ms), expiresAt: number (Unix ms), attempts: number, consumed: boolean}. TotpStore interface (implemented by consuming app): getSecret(userId: string): Promise<string> — returns Base32-encoded TOTP seed; storePendingChallenge(c: PendingChallenge): Promise<void>; getPendingChallenge(challengeId: string): Promise<PendingChallenge | null>; consumeChallenge(challengeId: string): Promise<void>. WebAuthnStore interface: getCredential(userId: string): Promise<WebAuthnCredential | null>; storeCredential(userId: string, credential: WebAuthnCredential): Promise<void>. SmsAdapter interface: send(phoneNumber: string, code: string): Promise<SmsDeliveryResult>. No database owned by the SDK.
target_output.api_design: Public TypeScript API: new MfaClient(config: MfaClientConfig) where MfaClientConfig = {totpStore?: TotpStore, webAuthnStore?: WebAuthnStore, smsAdapter?: SmsAdapter, webAuthnConfig?: {rpId: string, rpName: string, attestation?: 'direct' | 'none'}, fallbackPolicy?: MfaChannel[], otelTracer?: Tracer, testMode?: boolean}. mfaClient.challenge(userId: string, channel: MfaChannel): Promise<ChallengeResult>. mfaClient.verify(userId: string, channel: MfaChannel, code: string, challengeId: string): Promise<VerifyResult>. mfaClient.fallback(userId: string, opts: FallbackOptions): Promise<ChallengeResult>. mfaClient.registerWebAuthn(userId: string): Promise<RegistrationOptions>. mfaClient.verifyRegistration(userId: string, response: RegistrationResponseJSON): Promise<VerifyResult>. All methods return typed discriminated union results, never throw for expected error conditions.
target_output.security_and_compliance: TOTP: RFC 6238 HMAC-SHA1 with 30-second time step; ±1 window tolerance covering 90-second valid window; timingSafeEqual (Node.js) and SubtleCrypto.verify (browser) for constant-time code comparison preventing timing side-channels. WebAuthn: @simplewebauthn/server verifyRegistrationResponse and verifyAuthenticationResponse; attestation defaults to 'direct' in production, 'none' when testMode=true; 'none' attestation explicitly blocked in production to prevent credential forgery. SMS OTP: 6-digit code generated via crypto.getRandomValues (cryptographically secure); 5-minute expiry enforced by comparing ChallengeState.expiresAt to Date.now(); maximum 3 verify attempts per challenge; code not logged by SDK. NIST SP 800-63B AAL2 alignment: TOTP and WebAuthn meet AAL2 requirements; SMS OTP is AAL1 and fallback only. SDK documentation explicitly states that consuming app is responsible for secure TOTP seed storage and WebAuthn credential persistence.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Build pipeline: tsup --format cjs,esm --dts produces dist/index.cjs (Node.js CJS), dist/index.js (ESM), dist/index.d.ts (TypeScript declarations). package.json exports map: {'.' : {'import': './dist/index.js', 'require': './dist/index.cjs', 'types': './dist/index.d.ts'}}. @simplewebauthn/server listed under peerDependencies with optional: true and peerDependenciesMeta {'@simplewebauthn/server': {'optional': true}} to exclude from default install. GitHub Actions ci.yml: lint (eslint + prettier --check), type-check (tsc --noEmit), test (vitest --coverage --coverage.all --coverage.thresholds.lines=100), build (tsup), bundle-size (bundlesize checks gzipped ESM under 20480 bytes), api-surface-diff (@microsoft/api-extractor verify — fails on undocumented public API changes). GitHub Actions release.yml: triggered on version tag; runs changesets version, npm publish with provenance via NODE_AUTH_TOKEN, creates GitHub release with changelog. No infrastructure to operate — SDK runs in-process in consuming application.
target_output.observability: OpenTelemetry: SDK creates child spans under the consuming application's active trace context using @opentelemetry/api.trace.getActiveSpan().startActiveSpan(); span attributes: mfa.channel (totp/webauthn/sms), mfa.outcome (success/expired/invalid/replay/rate_limited), mfa.duration_ms. SDK no-ops gracefully if no tracer is configured in consuming app (checks for noop tracer). Consuming applications can use these spans to build MFA channel success rate and latency dashboards in their existing observability stack. No self-exporting telemetry from the SDK — consuming app owns the exporter configuration.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: No infrastructure costs for the SDK itself; it executes in-process. Publishing to npm is free for scoped packages under the organisation account. The in-memory pending_challenges Map scales within a single Node.js process; consuming applications with multiple instances must implement TotpStore backed by Redis or a similar shared cache — this is documented in the SDK README as a required step for distributed deployments. No compute or storage costs attributable to the SDK.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 — Alpha (Weeks 1-3): MfaClient scaffold, TotpChannel (RFC 6238 with SubtleCrypto/Node.js crypto dual-path), TotpStore adapter interface, challenge expiry and replay prevention, Vitest unit tests at 100% coverage on TOTP path, tsup build pipeline, npm alpha publish. Phase 2 — Beta (Weeks 4-6): SmsChannel with SmsAdapter interface, FallbackPolicy orchestration, OtelInstrumentation with child span emission, GitHub Actions CI pipeline with bundle-size and api-surface-diff checks, beta npm publish for consuming team integration testing. Phase 3 — GA (Weeks 7-8): WebAuthnChannel with @simplewebauthn/server, WebAuthnStore adapter interface, full three-channel integration tests, changesets release workflow, API documentation with TSDoc, 1.0.0 stable npm publish.
target_output.development_guidelines: All public API functions and interfaces must have TSDoc comments with @param, @returns, and @example tags — enforced by @microsoft/api-extractor in CI. TypeScript strict mode, exactOptionalPropertyTypes, and noUncheckedIndexedAccess are non-negotiable; no @ts-ignore or any types in public API surface. Adapter interfaces use async signatures throughout — synchronous implementations must wrap in Promise.resolve() in the consuming app, not the SDK. Vitest fake timers used for all TOTP window boundary tests to avoid flakiness from real clock drift. Branch protection: PR requires 1 approval and passing CI; no direct pushes to main.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: In-memory challenge state is lost on process restart: consuming applications that require cross-process or cross-restart challenge persistence must implement TotpStore with external storage — SDK documentation must make this prominent to avoid silent replay window extension. Tradeoff: @simplewebauthn/server as optional peer dependency keeps the core bundle under 20 KB but requires consuming app to install WebAuthn dep separately — acceptable for library SDK pattern. Timing-safe comparison is implemented per environment (timingSafeEqual on Node.js, SubtleCrypto.verify on browser); any future environment addition must implement the same pattern before merging.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Q1 (SMS retry logic): retry-with-backoff for SMS delivery failures is left to the consuming app via SmsAdapter implementation to avoid coupling SDK to a retry strategy that may conflict with consuming app's circuit breaker. Q2 (WebAuthn attestation defaults): 'direct' attestation required in production mode; 'none' permitted only when testMode=true to support integration testing without authenticator hardware. Q3 (challenge expiry storage): TotpStore must persist expiresAt to support multi-process deployments; in-memory Map used only as fallback for single-process setups.

metadata.notes: Round 1 first-pass for TypeScript MFA SDK. TOTP and adapter sections are detailed; cost_and_scaling is minimal as appropriate for a library SDK with no infrastructure. Overall quality is moderate — adequate detail across all sections without deep exhaustiveness.

profile.domain: A hardened desktop credential manager for security-conscious professionals and enterprise IT staff that stores passwords, OTP seeds, and SSH keys in an encrypted local vault protected by hardware-backed keys (TPM/Secure Enclave). Supports team vaults with audit logs and emergency-access delegation.

project_goal.value: Provide a hardened desktop credential manager storing passwords, OTP seeds, and SSH keys in a TPM/Secure Enclave-protected encrypted local vault with team vault sharing and audit logging
project_goal.rationale: Core security product replacing spreadsheet and sticky-note credential storage for enterprise IT teams

target_users.value: Security-conscious professionals and enterprise IT staff managing credentials across systems
target_users.rationale: Target personas identified in user research: sysadmins, DevOps engineers, security analysts

project_class.rationale: Packaged desktop application for macOS, Windows, Linux; no server-side runtime

capabilities.rationale: Tauri frontend, Rust IPC backend, SQLCipher data layer, hardware-backed auth

complexity_level.rationale: TPM/Secure Enclave key derivation, SQLCipher encryption, team vault sync, emergency access delegation

risk_level.rationale: Stores confidential credentials; compromise impacts entire credential estate

data_sensitivity.rationale: Vault contains passwords, OTP seeds, SSH private keys - highest sensitivity internal data

external_exposure.rationale: Vault data never leaves device; team sync uses E2EE with no server-side plaintext

access_model.value: Master key derived from TPM/Secure Enclave with biometric or PIN unlock; RBAC for team vault roles (owner, editor, viewer)
access_model.rationale: Hardware-backed key derivation prevents extraction of master key even with physical device access

feature_scope.value: Personal vault (passwords, OTP seeds, SSH keys), team vault sharing with E2EE, audit log with cryptographic proof, emergency access delegation with time-delay, SSH agent integration, browser extension autofill
feature_scope.rationale: Full feature set from enterprise security requirements

mvp_scope.value: Personal vault with TPM unlock, password and OTP seed storage, SQLCipher encryption, local audit log, auto-lock on idle
mvp_scope.rationale: Minimum for internal dog-fooding by IT security team

security_baseline.value: NIST SP 800-132 PBKDF2 fallback when TPM unavailable, AES-256-GCM vault encryption, argon2id for PIN stretching, vault MAC on every write
security_baseline.rationale: NIST SP 800-132 and FIPS 140-3 guidance for key derivation and storage

frontend_stack.value: Tauri with Svelte and TypeScript
frontend_stack.rationale: Tauri provides native OS integration (TPM, biometric, system keychain) with lightweight Svelte UI

backend_stack.rationale: Rust memory safety for credential handling; Axum for local IPC command dispatching

data_platform.value: SQLite encrypted with SQLCipher
data_platform.rationale: SQLCipher provides transparent AES-256-CBC page-level encryption with no plaintext vault on disk

hosting_target.value: Packaged desktop app for macOS, Windows, Linux
hosting_target.rationale: Distributed as signed installer; no cloud hosting required for local vault

privacy_retention_policy.value: All vault data stored locally; team vault sync encrypted end-to-end; audit logs retained for 2 years per corporate policy
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: No vault plaintext ever transmitted to servers; local data governed by device owner

future_scope.value: Browser extension autofill, SSH agent integration, FIDO2 hardware key as unlock factor, SOC 2 certification
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 features planned after internal deployment validates core vault

constraints.value: Local-only operation mandatory; vault file must be portable (USB backup); no cloud dependency for core vault function
constraints.rationale: Air-gapped environments require fully offline operation

observability_baseline.value: Local structured audit log to SQLite; crash reports via Sentry (no vault data in reports)
observability_baseline.rationale: Audit trail required for enterprise compliance; Sentry must be configured with data scrubbing

execution_preference.value: Iterative internal releases with IT security team dog-fooding each sprint
execution_preference.rationale: Security-sensitive product requires continuous internal review

compliance_context.value: NIST SP 800-132 key derivation, FIPS 140-3 cryptographic module guidance, corporate secret storage policy
compliance_context.rationale: Enterprise security policy mandates NIST-compliant key derivation and cryptographic controls

project.goal: Hardware-backed encrypted desktop credential manager with team vault sharing
project.target_users: Enterprise IT staff and security professionals

frontend.ui: native OS dialogs for biometric prompts

backend.framework: Axum IPC server on local Unix socket
backend.crypto: ring crate for AES-256-GCM, argon2 crate for PIN stretching

security.vault_key: TPM/Secure Enclave sealed master key, PBKDF2 fallback
security.audit: append-only audit_log_entries in separate SQLCipher DB

data.entities: vault_entries, otp_seeds, ssh_key_pairs, team_vaults, access_grants, audit_log_entries

devops.ci: GitHub Actions with cross-platform matrix (macOS, Windows, Linux)
devops.signing: Apple notarisation, Windows Authenticode, Linux GPG

constraints.vault_portability: vault file exportable for USB backup

reasoner_reviews.product: The MVP scope is correctly limited to personal vault with TPM unlock and local audit log. Browser extension and SSH agent integration are correctly deferred to phase 2 given the security review requirements they would introduce. Emergency access delegation is a differentiating feature that should be designed early even if deferred from MVP.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: The emergency access delegation design is the most architecturally complex feature: time-delayed approval requires a durable local timer that survives app restarts, and the delegation handoff must use asymmetric encryption so the vault owner's key is not shared. The team vault E2EE sync needs an explicit key agreement protocol defined; using Signal Protocol's double ratchet would be over-engineered; a simpler ECDH per-member encryption of the team vault key suffices.
reasoner_reviews.security: The audit log must be in a separate SQLCipher database from the vault to ensure an attacker who compromises the vault key cannot also delete or modify the audit trail. All audit_log_entries must include a cryptographic chain hash (each entry hashes the previous entry's hash plus its own content) to detect tampering. Vault MAC verification must run on every open, not just on unlock.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Tauri cross-compilation matrix on GitHub Actions requires separate build jobs for macOS (aarch64+x86_64 universal), Windows (x86_64), and Linux (x86_64+arm64). Apple notarisation adds ~10min to macOS release pipeline. SQLCipher portability is well-established; vault file is self-contained and USB-transportable.
reasoner_reviews.critic: Round 2 plan improved the audit log chaining design but still did not specify the emergency access time-delay mechanism concretely. The timer must survive app restarts and the approval window must be enforced even if the app is closed between request and approval.

backend.service_design: Rust Axum IPC server on local Unix domain socket (macOS/Linux) or named pipe (Windows). Command handlers: VaultCommand (unlock, lock, create_entry, read_entry, delete_entry), TeamVaultCommand (share, revoke, list_members), EmergencyAccessCommand (request_access, approve_access, deny_access), AuditCommand (query_log). vault_service module owns SQLCipher connection and in-memory decrypted vault state.
backend.api_patterns: Tauri invoke() calls map to Rust commands via Tauri command macros. IPC request types are Rust enums serialised with serde_json. vault_service::read_entry returns DecryptedEntry with plaintext field zeroed after use via ZeroizingVec.
backend.failure_handling: Vault auto-locks after 15-minute idle timeout enforced by Tauri window event listener. Failed biometric prompt falls back to PIN with argon2id stretching. SQLCipher write failures trigger vault integrity check before any further reads.
backend.scaling_notes: Single-user local vault supports up to 10,000 entries without query performance issues. Team vault sync is P2P encrypted blob exchange; no central server bottleneck.

frontend.app_structure: Tauri Svelte SPA with views: VaultUnlock, VaultEntryList, EntryDetail (password/OTP/SSH), TeamVaultPanel, EmergencyAccessPanel, AuditLogViewer, Settings.
frontend.pages_and_flows: VaultUnlock triggers OS biometric dialog via Tauri plugin; on success calls vault:unlock command. EntryDetail for OTP seeds shows TOTP code with countdown timer via WASM TOTP implementation. SSH key entries display public key fingerprint only; private key accessed only via copy-to-clipboard with 30s clipboard clear.
frontend.state_management: Svelte stores: vaultLocked (bool), entries (Map of entry_id to VaultEntry metadata), selectedEntryId. Plaintext fields never stored in Svelte state; fetched on-demand via IPC and zeroed after display.
frontend.frontend_security_notes: Clipboard auto-clear after 30s for passwords and OTP codes. Screen capture protection via Tauri window flag on sensitive entry views. No vault data written to localStorage or sessionStorage.

security.auth_design: Master key derivation: TPM Seal/Unseal on macOS (Secure Enclave via CryptoKit) and Windows (TPM 2.0 via NCrypt). Fallback: argon2id(PIN, salt=device_id_hash, m=65536, t=3, p=4) producing 32-byte key. Vault key wrapped with master key using AES-256-GCM; wrapped key stored in vault metadata header.
security.authorization_model: Team vault RBAC: owner (full CRUD + member management), editor (CRUD on entries), viewer (read-only). Permissions enforced in Rust vault_service before any SQLCipher access.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: Separate audit SQLCipher DB at audit.db. audit_log_entries: id UUID, action ENUM unlock/lock/read_entry/write_entry/delete_entry/share_vault/emergency_access_request/emergency_access_approved, entry_id nullable UUID, actor_identity string, timestamp ISO8601, prev_hash SHA256 of previous row, row_hash SHA256(prev_hash + action + entry_id + actor_identity + timestamp). Chain verified on audit DB open.
security.security_risks: Risk: TPM unavailability on VM or containers falls back to PIN-only. Mitigation: argon2id parameters enforced; PIN complexity requirements enforced in UI. Risk: vault file theft with known PIN. Mitigation: argon2id makes brute-force computationally expensive; vault key wrapped in hardware on TPM-capable devices.

data.entities: vault_entries (id UUID PK, entry_type ENUM password/otp_seed/ssh_key, title VARCHAR, username VARCHAR, url VARCHAR, notes_encrypted BLOB, ciphertext BLOB, created_at DATETIME, updated_at DATETIME). otp_seeds (id UUID PK, entry_id FK, seed_encrypted BLOB, issuer VARCHAR, digits INT DEFAULT 6, period INT DEFAULT 30). ssh_key_pairs (id UUID PK, entry_id FK, private_key_encrypted BLOB, public_key TEXT, key_type ENUM ed25519/rsa4096, fingerprint VARCHAR). team_vaults (id UUID PK, name VARCHAR, owner_id VARCHAR, created_at DATETIME). access_grants (id UUID PK, vault_id FK, grantee_identity VARCHAR, role ENUM owner/editor/viewer, encrypted_vault_key BLOB, granted_at DATETIME). audit_log_entries (id UUID PK, action ENUM, entry_id UUID nullable, actor_identity VARCHAR, timestamp DATETIME, prev_hash VARCHAR, row_hash VARCHAR).
data.storage_design: vault.db encrypted with SQLCipher AES-256-CBC; page_size=4096; key derived from master key. audit.db separate SQLCipher DB with independent key derived from master key XOR audit_key_salt stored in secure enclave.
data.schema_notes: All ciphertext BLOBs store AES-256-GCM nonce prepended to ciphertext. Vault MAC computed over all entry_ids and their updated_at timestamps; stored in vault_metadata table; verified on every open.
data.migration_notes: SQLCipher schema migrations via embedded Rust migration runner; each migration wrapped in transaction; migration log in vault_metadata.

devops.deployment_topology: GitHub Actions matrix: macOS universal binary (lipo x86_64+aarch64), Windows x86_64 MSVC, Linux x86_64+arm64 AppImage and .deb. Code-signed on each platform: Apple notarisation, Windows Authenticode via Azure Code Signing, Linux GPG.
devops.ci_cd_design: Jobs: lint (clippy + eslint), test (cargo test + vitest), build (tauri build per platform), sign+notarise (macOS only on main), package (AppImage, .deb, .exe, .dmg). Release artifacts uploaded to GitHub Releases. Auto-update via Tauri updater with signature check.
devops.rollback_strategy: Vault schema migrations are additive-only; rollback to previous app version always succeeds on existing vault schema.
devops.devops_risks: Apple notarisation requires internet access in CI; build fails in air-gapped CI runners. Mitigation: notarisation only on release tag builds, not on every commit.

ISS-001.title: Emergency access time-delay timer does not survive app restarts
ISS-001.detail: Round 2 plan specified a 24-hour approval window for emergency access but did not describe how the timer persists across app restarts. If the app is closed between the access request and the approval window expiry, the timer is lost.

history.note: Identified in architect_reasoner review
Round 2 plan mentioned 24h window but did not specify persistence mechanism

ISS-002.title: Audit log in same SQLCipher DB as vault allows key-compromise to destroy audit trail
ISS-002.detail: Round 1 stored audit_log_entries in vault.db; round 2 moved audit to separate audit.db with independent key derivation

history.note: Security review: audit and vault must use separate encryption keys
Round 2 separated audit.db with XOR-derived key

ISS-003.title: Vault MAC not verified on every open, only on unlock
ISS-003.detail: Round 1 plan verified vault MAC only on initial unlock; round 2 plan added MAC verification on every vault open command

history.note: Round 2 added MAC check to every open operation

focus_issues.title: Emergency access time-delay timer does not survive app restarts
focus_issues.detail: Emergency access approval window is lost when app is closed, defeating the time-delay security guarantee.

latest_recommendations: Persist emergency access request with requested_at timestamp in audit.db; on app start check all pending requests against current time to enforce window without in-memory timer
Use asymmetric ECDH for emergency vault key handoff so vault owner key is never shared with delegate
Document timer persistence and expiry check logic concretely in workflows section

convergence_state.reason: Emergency access timer persistence unresolved after two rounds

previous_audits.summary: Round 2 resolved the critical audit log separation and vault MAC issues. The emergency access time-delay design remains incomplete and is a high-severity gap for the team vault feature.
strengths: Separate audit.db with XOR-derived key is a well-designed audit trail protection
Vault MAC on every open prevents silent tampering between sessions
SQLCipher AES-256-CBC with argon2id fallback is NIST SP 800-132 compliant
concerns: Emergency access approval timer lost on app restart
ECDH key agreement for team vault key sharing not yet specified
Tauri screen capture protection flag not documented for sensitive views
blocking_issues: Emergency access timer must persist across app restarts using timestamp-based persistence in audit.db
recommendations: Store emergency_access_requests as rows in audit.db with requested_at DATETIME; on app start query all pending requests and evaluate (now - requested_at) >= approval_delay_hours
Use ECDH (X25519) for encrypting team vault key to each member's public key; never store vault key in plaintext on sync server
Document screen capture protection Tauri window flag in frontend_security_notes

issue_updates.title: Emergency access time-delay timer does not survive app restarts
issue_updates.detail: Round 2 plan described a 24h window but relied on in-memory timer that resets on restart

previous_plan.title: Hardened Desktop Credential Manager - Round 2
previous_plan.executive_summary: Tauri+Svelte desktop app with Rust Axum IPC backend storing credentials in SQLCipher vault. Round 2 separated audit.db and added vault MAC on every open.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: Master key via TPM/Secure Enclave, argon2id PIN fallback. Separate audit.db with XOR-derived key and cryptographic hash chain. Vault MAC on every open. Emergency access 24h window - timer not persisted across restarts.
previous_plan.data_model: vault_entries, otp_seeds, ssh_key_pairs, team_vaults, access_grants in vault.db. audit_log_entries in audit.db with hash chain.
previous_plan.workflows: Vault unlock via biometric. Entry CRUD with AES-256-GCM per-entry encryption. Emergency access request creates 24h in-memory timer.

target_output.thinking_summary: Resolving ISS-001 by replacing the in-memory emergency access timer with a persistent timestamp-based approach: emergency_access_requests stored as rows in audit.db with requested_at and approval_delay_hours. On every app start, the vault_service queries pending emergency_access_requests and evaluates (now - requested_at) >= approval_delay_hours; if elapsed, the access is auto-approved or expired depending on owner response. This design is restart-safe because the state lives in audit.db, not in memory. Also specifying X25519 ECDH for team vault key handoff to close the key sharing gap from round 2.

fix_report.action_taken: Replaced in-memory emergency access timer with persistent emergency_access_requests table in audit.db storing requested_at DATETIME and approval_delay_hours INT. On app start, vault_service queries SELECT * FROM emergency_access_requests WHERE status = 'pending' and evaluates (julianday('now') - julianday(requested_at)) * 24 >= approval_delay_hours. Expired pending requests auto-resolve. Notifications sent to vault owner via OS notification on app start if action required.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Emergency access approval window is enforced correctly even after multiple app restarts between request submission and expiry, preserving the security guarantee of the time-delay mechanism.

target_output.title: Hardened Desktop Credential Manager
target_output.executive_summary: A Tauri and Svelte desktop application with a Rust Axum IPC backend storing passwords, OTP seeds, and SSH keys in SQLCipher-encrypted local vaults. Master key is sealed to device TPM or Secure Enclave; fallback uses argon2id PIN stretching. Team vault sharing uses X25519 ECDH per-member key encryption. Emergency access requests persist across app restarts via audit.db timestamp tracking. Distributed as signed installers for macOS, Windows, and Linux.
target_output.architecture_overview: Local-only desktop architecture: Tauri shell hosts Svelte TypeScript UI and communicates with a Rust Axum command handler via Tauri invoke() over a local Unix domain socket (macOS/Linux) or named pipe (Windows). vault_service module holds in-memory decrypted vault state and owns two SQLCipher databases: vault.db for credential entries and audit.db for append-only audit log and emergency access state. Master key derived from TPM Seal/Unseal (Secure Enclave on macOS, NCrypt TPM 2.0 on Windows) or argon2id from PIN as fallback. No network calls required for core vault; team sync uses direct E2EE blob exchange.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: Tauri 2.x, Svelte 4, TypeScript strict mode, Tauri plugin for biometric and OS keychain. Backend: Rust stable, Axum 0.7, SQLCipher via rusqlite with sqlcipher feature, ring crate (AES-256-GCM), argon2 crate (argon2id), x25519-dalek (team vault ECDH). Build: GitHub Actions cross-platform matrix. Packaging: tauri-bundler (macOS dmg universal, Windows NSIS, Linux AppImage and .deb). Crash reporting: Sentry Rust SDK with data scrubbing.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Personal Vault: create/read/update/delete entries of type password, otp_seed, ssh_key with AES-256-GCM per-entry encryption. TOTP Generator: computes TOTP code from decrypted otp_seed with 30s countdown display; clipboard cleared after 30s. SSH Key Viewer: displays public key fingerprint; private key accessible only via copy-to-clipboard. Team Vault: share vault key to member using X25519 ECDH; member roles owner/editor/viewer enforced in Rust. Emergency Access: request access with configurable delay (24-72h); request persisted in audit.db; owner approves or denies on next app open within window. Audit Log Viewer: query audit_log_entries with hash chain integrity check.
target_output.system_components: Tauri shell: window management, biometric plugin (macOS LocalAuthentication, Windows Windows Hello), OS notification. Svelte UI: VaultUnlock, EntryList, EntryDetail, TeamVaultPanel, EmergencyAccessPanel, AuditLogViewer. Rust vault_service: SQLCipher pool for vault.db and audit.db, in-memory decrypted entry cache (zeroed on lock), emergency access request evaluator running on startup. ring crate: AES-256-GCM encrypt/decrypt, HKDF for key derivation. argon2 crate: PIN stretching. x25519-dalek: team vault ECDH key agreement. audit_service: append-only writes with hash chain computation.
target_output.workflows: Vault Unlock: Tauri invokes biometric OS dialog; on success calls CryptoKit/NCrypt to unseal master key from TPM; vault_service opens vault.db and audit.db with SQLCipher key derived from master key; verifies vault MAC; queries emergency_access_requests for pending items requiring owner action; emits OS notification if action needed. Emergency Access Request: requestor sends request with requested_at = now() stored in emergency_access_requests table (audit.db) with status=pending and approval_delay_hours; vault owner receives OS notification on next app open; if (now - requested_at) >= approval_delay_hours and owner has not denied, vault_service generates time-limited access token encrypted with requestor's public key. Team Vault Key Share: owner calls EmergencyAccessCommand::share; vault_service generates ephemeral X25519 keypair, derives shared_secret = ECDH(owner_private, member_public), encrypts vault_key with AES-256-GCM using shared_secret, stores encrypted_vault_key in access_grants.
target_output.data_model: vault.db - vault_entries (id UUID PK, entry_type ENUM password/otp_seed/ssh_key, title VARCHAR, username VARCHAR, url VARCHAR, nonce BLOB 12 bytes, ciphertext BLOB, created_at DATETIME, updated_at DATETIME). otp_seeds (id UUID PK, entry_id FK, nonce BLOB, seed_encrypted BLOB, issuer VARCHAR, digits INT DEFAULT 6, period INT DEFAULT 30). ssh_key_pairs (id UUID PK, entry_id FK, nonce BLOB, private_key_encrypted BLOB, public_key TEXT, key_type ENUM ed25519/rsa4096, fingerprint CHAR 47). team_vaults (id UUID PK, name VARCHAR, owner_identity VARCHAR, created_at DATETIME). access_grants (id UUID PK, vault_id FK, grantee_identity VARCHAR, role ENUM owner/editor/viewer, ecdh_wrapped_vault_key BLOB, granted_at DATETIME). vault_metadata (key VARCHAR PK, value TEXT) holding vault_mac and schema_version. audit.db - audit_log_entries (id UUID PK, action ENUM, entry_id UUID nullable, actor_identity VARCHAR, timestamp DATETIME, prev_hash CHAR 64, row_hash CHAR 64). emergency_access_requests (id UUID PK, requestor_identity VARCHAR, requestor_public_key BLOB, approval_delay_hours INT, requested_at DATETIME, status ENUM pending/approved/denied/expired, resolved_at DATETIME nullable).
target_output.api_design: Tauri invoke commands - vault:unlock(pin_or_biometric) -> VaultStatus. vault:create_entry(entry_type, fields) -> EntryId. vault:read_entry(entry_id) -> DecryptedEntry (zeroed after use). vault:delete_entry(entry_id) -> void. team:share_vault(vault_id, member_identity, member_public_key, role) -> GrantId. team:revoke_member(grant_id) -> void. emergency:request_access(vault_id, requestor_public_key, delay_hours) -> RequestId. emergency:approve_access(request_id) -> EncryptedAccessToken. emergency:deny_access(request_id) -> void. audit:query_log(filters) -> AuditLogPage with chain_valid bool.
target_output.security_and_compliance: NIST SP 800-132 compliance: master key derived via PBKDF2-HMAC-SHA256 on PIN fallback with 600,000 iterations (FIPS 140-3 guidance). TPM path: Secure Enclave CryptoKit on macOS, NCrypt TPM 2.0 on Windows. Per-entry encryption: AES-256-GCM with unique 96-bit nonce per write. Vault MAC: HMAC-SHA256 over all (entry_id, updated_at) pairs; recomputed and verified on every vault open. Audit hash chain: row_hash = SHA256(prev_hash || action || entry_id || actor_identity || timestamp); chain verified on every audit.db open. Emergency access timer: persisted in audit.db; app-restart-safe. Team vault key sharing: X25519 ECDH shared secret -> AES-256-GCM encrypted vault key; owner private key never transmitted. Clipboard cleared after 30s. Screen capture protection on sensitive views via Tauri setContentProtection(true).
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions matrix: [macos-14 (aarch64), macos-13 (x86_64)], [windows-2022], [ubuntu-22.04, ubuntu-22.04-arm64]. Steps: clippy lint, cargo test (unit + integration with in-memory SQLCipher), vitest for Svelte components, tauri build, lipo universal binary for macOS. Release tags trigger: Apple notarisation + staple (macOS), Azure Code Signing (Windows), GPG sign AppImage (Linux). Artifacts uploaded to GitHub Releases. Auto-update manifest signed with Tauri updater key pair.
target_output.observability: Local audit log in audit.db with hash chain provides tamper-evident access history. Sentry Rust SDK for crash reports: DSN configured in Tauri conf; data scrubbing rules strip any string matching UUID patterns from stack frames. App version and OS version sent with crashes; no vault data. Structured tracing logs via tracing crate (info level) written to local log file with 30-day rotation.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Zero hosting cost for core vault; local-only. GitHub Actions free tier covers cross-platform build matrix. Apple Developer Program ($99/yr) required for notarisation. Windows EV certificate for Authenticode (~$300/yr). SQLCipher handles up to 10,000 entries with sub-50ms query time on consumer hardware.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (8 weeks): personal vault unlock via TPM/PIN, password and OTP seed CRUD with AES-256-GCM, local audit.db with hash chain, auto-lock on 15min idle, signed installers for macOS and Windows. Phase 2 (6 weeks): SSH key storage, team vault sharing with X25519 ECDH, emergency access with persistent timer, team RBAC. Phase 3 (4 weeks): browser extension autofill, SSH agent integration, FIDO2 hardware key unlock factor.
target_output.development_guidelines: Rust: clippy deny(warnings), cargo test with coverage via cargo-tarpaulin targeting 85% line coverage. Secrets never in Rust String type; use ZeroizeVec from zeroize crate for plaintext buffers. Svelte: TypeScript strict, vitest for component logic. No vault plaintext in Svelte stores; fetch on-demand via IPC. PR requires security team review for any change to vault_service or audit_service modules.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: TPM unavailable in VM environments; argon2id fallback with enforced PIN complexity mitigates brute-force. Risk: vault file loss without backup; mitigated by prompted portable export to encrypted USB. Tradeoff: SQLCipher over age-encrypted flat file chosen for relational query capability needed by audit log and team vault features. Risk: Apple notarisation delay blocking release; mitigated by pre-notarising release candidates before tag.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: X25519 ECDH chosen over Signal double ratchet for team vault key sharing; double ratchet is over-engineered for vault key exchange which does not require forward secrecy on every message. Emergency access timer persistence chosen via audit.db rows rather than OS scheduled tasks to maintain single source of truth and audit trail. argon2id parameters set to m=65536, t=3, p=4 following OWASP recommendation for interactive logins.

metadata.notes: Round 3 revision resolving emergency access timer persistence ISS-001 for hardened desktop credential manager with TPM-backed vault.

profile.domain: An automation tool that modernises legacy LDAP group-based access control by migrating group definitions and memberships into an ABAC policy engine, replacing flat group strings with structured attribute sets and generating equivalent Rego policies for validation.

project_goal.value: Automate migration of legacy LDAP group-based access control into an ABAC policy engine, generating Rego policies from attribute mappings and validating them against known allow/deny decisions
project_goal.rationale: Manual migration of 3000+ LDAP groups is error-prone and would take 6+ months; automation reduces timeline to 4 weeks

target_users.value: Internal IAM engineers and platform security team performing the LDAP-to-ABAC migration
target_users.rationale: Tool is used by a small team of IAM specialists; not end-user facing

project_class.rationale: CLI-driven migration tool; no persistent hosted service

capabilities.rationale: LDAP integration, SQLite state store, batch policy generation and validation

complexity_level.rationale: LDAP enumeration, heuristic attribute inference, Rego generation, and regression testing pipeline

risk_level.rationale: Incorrect Rego policy generation could grant unintended access or block legitimate users in production

data_sensitivity.rationale: Group memberships are internal organizational data; no customer PII

external_exposure.rationale: Tool runs on internal network with LDAP bind credentials; no public exposure

access_model.value: LDAP bind credentials stored in environment variables; tool runs under IAM engineer user account; no end-user access model
access_model.rationale: CLI tool invoked manually by IAM team during migration window

feature_scope.value: LDAP enumeration and group dump, heuristic attribute inference with human review, Rego policy generation, regression test suite execution, migration report generation
feature_scope.rationale: Full migration workflow from LDAP discovery to validated Rego output

mvp_scope.value: LDAP group enumeration, attribute mapping file, Rego policy generation, basic allow/deny test suite, migration_report.json output
mvp_scope.rationale: Minimum to process first 100 LDAP groups for pilot validation

security_baseline.value: LDAP credentials only in environment variables, never in config files; generated Rego files reviewed by human before deployment; dry-run mode default
security_baseline.rationale: ISO 27001 A.9 access control change management requires human review of policy changes

frontend_stack.value: N/A - CLI tool with no frontend
frontend_stack.rationale: automation_tool project class; all interaction via Cobra CLI flags and stdout

backend_stack.rationale: Go concurrency for parallel LDAP queries; Cobra for subcommand structure

data_platform.value: SQLite for migration state store
data_platform.rationale: Portable local state for incremental migration runs; no database server required

hosting_target.value: Internal tooling server, self-hosted
hosting_target.rationale: Runs on IAM engineer workstation or dedicated migration host during migration window

privacy_retention_policy.value: Migration state in SQLite retained for audit; LDAP credentials never persisted; generated Rego files retained in version control
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: ISO 27001 A.9 requires change audit trail for access control modifications

future_scope.value: OPA playground integration for interactive policy testing, Terraform provider for automated Rego deployment, continuous drift detection comparing LDAP changes against ABAC policies
future_scope.rationale: Post-migration steady-state tooling items

constraints.value: Must operate against read-only LDAP bind; no writes to LDAP; Rego output must be compatible with OPA 0.60+; dry-run mode required
constraints.rationale: Read-only LDAP bind limits blast radius; dry-run required for safety

observability_baseline.value: Structured log output (zap logger) to stdout and migration.log; progress bars for long LDAP enumeration; migration_report.json with per-group status
observability_baseline.rationale: IAM team needs visibility into which groups have been processed and which have validation regressions

execution_preference.value: Incremental runs: process N groups per run, resume from last checkpoint in SQLite
execution_preference.rationale: 3000+ LDAP groups cannot all be processed in a single run; incremental resumption needed

compliance_context.value: ISO 27001 A.9 access control change management; internal access review policy requiring human approval before Rego deployment
compliance_context.rationale: ISO 27001 certification requires documented change management for access control modifications

project.goal: CLI tool migrating LDAP groups to ABAC Rego policies with validation and migration reports
project.target_users: IAM engineers

backend.framework: Cobra CLI with subcommands: enumerate, map-attributes, generate, validate, report

security.credentials: LDAP_BIND_DN and LDAP_BIND_PASSWORD from environment only
security.dry_run: default mode; --apply required for state writes
security.rego_review: generated files not deployed without human sign-off

data.entities: ldap_groups, group_memberships, attribute_mappings, abac_policies, rego_policy_files, migration_reports

devops.ci: Jenkins pipeline: go vet, go test, go build

reasoner_reviews.product: The incremental run design with SQLite checkpointing is correctly specified; 3000 LDAP groups cannot be processed atomically. The dry-run default is essential for a tool with ISO 27001 change management requirements. The migration_report.json needs a machine-readable format for integration with the IAM team's change management ticketing system.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: The Rego regression test suite is the highest-value feature: it catches policy regressions before deployment. The test fixture format needs to be concrete. OPA eval in subprocess is the correct approach for validating Rego files without embedding OPA as a Go library. The attribute mapping heuristic should produce a candidate_mappings.yaml that humans review and approve before Rego generation proceeds.
reasoner_reviews.security: The dry-run default and --apply flag requirement are the correct safety controls. Generated Rego files must be written to an output directory, not auto-applied; the IAM team reviews them in a PR before OPA deployment. The migration state in SQLite must record which groups have been reviewed and approved to prevent re-processing approved groups in subsequent runs.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Go build for Linux amd64 is straightforward on Jenkins. The go-ldap/ldap/v3 library supports read-only bind and LDAP pagination for large group enumerations. OPA subprocess approach requires OPA binary present on PATH in migration environment; document this prerequisite.
reasoner_reviews.critic: Round 3 plan improved the Rego generation but the attribute mapping review workflow is still not concrete. The tool generates candidate_mappings.yaml but there is no command to mark a mapping as human-approved, so subsequent runs cannot distinguish approved mappings from unreviewed ones. This means re-running the tool might overwrite approved mappings with regenerated candidates.

backend.service_design: Cobra CLI with subcommands: enumerate (LDAP group and member discovery), map-attributes (heuristic inference + human review workflow), generate (Rego policy file generation), validate (OPA regression test execution), report (migration_report.json generation). SQLite state store accessed via internal store package wrapping database/sql.
backend.api_patterns: CLI flags: --ldap-url, --base-dn, --output-dir, --batch-size (default 100), --apply (required to write SQLite state beyond dry-run), --opa-binary (path to OPA executable). Subcommands are idempotent: re-running enumerate updates group_memberships rows without duplicating; re-running generate skips groups with status=rego_approved.
backend.background_jobs: enumerate subcommand uses goroutine pool (default 8 workers) to parallelize LDAP search requests for group member enumeration. Progress reported via mpb progress bar. Groups are checkpointed to SQLite in batches of 100 after each worker completes.
backend.failure_handling: LDAP connection failure retries 3 times with exponential backoff. SQLite write failures abort the current batch and roll back the transaction; the run can be resumed from the last successful checkpoint. OPA subprocess failures (non-zero exit, stderr output) are captured and included in migration_report.json as validation_errors.

security.auth_design: LDAP bind credentials read from LDAP_BIND_DN and LDAP_BIND_PASSWORD environment variables at startup; tool exits with error if not set. Credentials never logged (zap logger uses a custom core that redacts values matching the bind password string). LDAPS (ldap over TLS) used by default; --ldap-insecure flag required to fall back to plaintext LDAP.
security.secrets_management: No credentials persisted to SQLite or log files. Generated Rego files contain no credentials. candidate_mappings.yaml contains no credentials; only group DNs and inferred attribute names.
security.security_risks: Risk: generated Rego policies with incorrect allow rules granting over-broad access. Mitigation: regression test suite must pass 100% before any Rego file is marked rego_approved; human review required before OPA deployment.

data.entities: ldap_groups (id INTEGER PK, dn VARCHAR UNIQUE, cn VARCHAR, description VARCHAR, member_count INT, enumerated_at DATETIME, status ENUM enumerated/mapped/rego_generated/rego_validated/rego_approved). group_memberships (id INTEGER PK, group_id FK, member_dn VARCHAR, member_type ENUM user/group, discovered_at DATETIME). attribute_mappings (id INTEGER PK, group_id FK, attribute_key VARCHAR, attribute_value VARCHAR, inferred_by ENUM heuristic/manual, reviewed_by VARCHAR nullable, reviewed_at DATETIME nullable, status ENUM candidate/approved/rejected). abac_policies (id INTEGER PK, group_id FK, policy_name VARCHAR, effect ENUM allow/deny, action VARCHAR, resource_pattern VARCHAR). rego_policy_files (id INTEGER PK, group_id FK, file_path VARCHAR, content_hash VARCHAR, generated_at DATETIME, validated_at DATETIME nullable, validation_passed BOOL nullable). migration_reports (id INTEGER PK, run_at DATETIME, groups_total INT, groups_enumerated INT, groups_mapped INT, groups_validated INT, groups_approved INT, validation_errors_json TEXT).
data.storage_design: Single SQLite file migration.db created in --output-dir. WAL mode enabled for concurrent reads during long enumeration runs. Group processing is transactional: each group's state transition (enumerated -> mapped -> rego_generated) committed atomically.
data.consistency_notes: Idempotency: enumerate INSERT OR REPLACE on ldap_groups keyed by dn. generate skips groups where status IN (rego_approved) to prevent overwriting approved policies. approve-mapping subcommand sets attribute_mappings.status=approved and reviewed_by=current_user.

devops.deployment_topology: Single Go binary compiled for linux/amd64. Distributed as tarball to internal tooling server. OPA binary (0.60+) must be present on PATH. SQLite migration.db and output Rego files stored in --output-dir on local filesystem.
devops.ci_cd_design: Jenkins pipeline: go vet, go test -race -coverprofile (fail under 80% coverage), go build -o ldap-abac-migrator, archive binary as Jenkins artifact. Integration test job runs against a containerised OpenLDAP fixture with known group structure and validates Rego output against expected files.
devops.observability_stack: zap structured logger writing JSON to stdout and migration.log in --output-dir. mpb progress bar for enumerate and validate subcommands. migration_report.json written to --output-dir on every report subcommand invocation with per-group status breakdown.
devops.devops_risks: Risk: Jenkins runner not having OPA binary on PATH breaks validate subcommand in CI. Mitigation: CI step downloads OPA binary to workspace and sets PATH before test execution.

ISS-001.title: approve-mapping subcommand not specified; re-runs overwrite approved mappings
ISS-001.detail: Rounds 1-3 did not include a mechanism for IAM engineers to mark attribute_mappings as human-approved. Each re-run of map-attributes regenerates candidate mappings from heuristics, potentially overwriting entries that had been manually corrected.

Round 2 added candidate_mappings.yaml but no approval tracking
Round 3 added reviewed_by column but no approve-mapping CLI command

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 4 resolves ISS-001 by adding the approve-mapping subcommand and the corresponding skip logic in map-attributes. The approve-mapping command sets attribute_mappings.status=approved for all mappings of a group and records reviewed_by and reviewed_at. The map-attributes subcommand now checks: if all attribute_mappings for a group have status=approved, skip regeneration and log a skip notice. This makes re-runs safe. Plan quality is moderate per manifest.

fix_report.action_taken: Added approve-mapping {group-dn} subcommand that sets attribute_mappings.status=approved, reviewed_by=$USER (or --reviewer flag), reviewed_at=now() for all candidate mappings of the specified group DN. Updated map-attributes to query SELECT COUNT(*) FROM attribute_mappings WHERE group_id=? AND status='candidate' before regeneration; if count=0 (all approved or none candidate), log 'skipping group {dn}: all mappings approved' and continue to next group. Added approved_mappings_count and pending_review_count fields to migration_report.json.
fix_report.expected_outcome: IAM engineers can safely re-run map-attributes without losing manually approved attribute corrections. The migration_report.json provides a clear count of approved vs pending mappings for change management tracking.

target_output.title: LDAP to ABAC Migration Tool
target_output.executive_summary: A Go 1.22 Cobra CLI tool for IAM engineers to migrate 3000+ legacy LDAP groups to ABAC Rego policies. Subcommands enumerate LDAP groups and memberships, infer attribute mappings with human approval workflow, generate OPA-compatible Rego policy files, run regression test suites against known allow/deny decisions, and produce machine-readable migration reports. State is checkpointed in SQLite for incremental resumption across runs.
target_output.architecture_overview: Single Go binary run by IAM engineers on an internal tooling host. Cobra CLI dispatches to domain packages: ldap (enumeration), mapper (attribute inference), generator (Rego template rendering), validator (OPA subprocess execution), store (SQLite state), reporter (JSON report generation). LDAP enumeration uses parallel goroutine pool against read-only bind. Generated Rego files are written to --output-dir for human review and PR-based deployment; the tool itself never applies policies to OPA. SQLite migration.db tracks group processing state through the full pipeline: enumerated -> mapped -> rego_generated -> rego_validated -> rego_approved.
target_output.technology_stack: Language: Go 1.22. CLI framework: Cobra. LDAP client: go-ldap/ldap/v3 with LDAPS and pagination support. Database: database/sql with mattn/go-sqlite3, WAL mode. Rego generation: text/template with OPA-compatible output. Validation: exec.Command invoking OPA eval subprocess. Logging: zap with custom redaction core. Progress: mpb progress bars. Build: go build -o ldap-abac-migrator linux/amd64. CI: Jenkins with containerised OpenLDAP test fixture.
target_output.functional_feature_map: enumerate: connects to LDAP with LDAPS, paginates through all groups under --base-dn, resolves member DNs, writes ldap_groups and group_memberships to SQLite, checkpoints every 100 groups. map-attributes: for each group without approved mappings, runs heuristic inference (CN word tokenisation, description keyword matching, nested group expansion) producing attribute_mappings rows with status=candidate; skips groups where all mappings are status=approved. approve-mapping: sets all attribute_mappings for a group DN to status=approved with reviewed_by and reviewed_at; enables re-run safety. generate: for each group with status=mapped, renders Rego policy file from abac_policies rows using text/template, writes to --output-dir/{group_cn}.rego, updates rego_policy_files. validate: runs OPA eval on each .rego file against a test fixture of known allow/deny decisions, records pass/fail in rego_policy_files, blocks groups with validation failures. report: writes migration_report.json with per-group status, approved_mappings_count, pending_review_count, validation_errors.
target_output.system_components: Cobra root command with subcommands: enumerate, map-attributes, approve-mapping, generate, validate, report. ldap package: LDAPClient wrapping go-ldap/ldap/v3, group and member pagination. mapper package: AttributeInferrer with CN tokeniser and description keyword extractor, ApprovalStore methods. generator package: RegoGenerator using text/template rendering abac_policies to OPA allow rules. validator package: OPARunner exec.Command subprocess with stdout/stderr capture. store package: SQLite repository for all entity CRUD with prepared statements. reporter package: MigrationReport struct marshalled to JSON.
target_output.workflows: Full Migration Pipeline: (1) IAM engineer sets LDAP_BIND_DN and LDAP_BIND_PASSWORD env vars. (2) Runs ldap-abac-migrator enumerate --ldap-url ldaps://ldap.corp:636 --base-dn ou=groups,dc=corp,dc=com --output-dir ./migration --apply; processes 3000 groups in batches with progress bar. (3) Runs map-attributes --apply; tool infers candidate attribute mappings for all unapproved groups; engineer reviews candidate_mappings section of output-dir. (4) Engineer runs approve-mapping --group-dn cn=net-admins,ou=groups,dc=corp,dc=com --reviewer alice for each reviewed group; tool marks mappings approved. (5) Runs generate --apply; creates .rego files in output-dir for all approved groups. (6) Runs validate; OPA eval runs against test fixtures; any group with regression is flagged in migration_report.json. (7) Engineer opens PR with .rego files for IAM lead review before OPA deployment.
target_output.data_model: ldap_groups (id INTEGER PK, dn VARCHAR UNIQUE, cn VARCHAR, description VARCHAR, member_count INT, enumerated_at DATETIME, status ENUM enumerated/mapped/rego_generated/rego_validated/rego_approved). group_memberships (id INTEGER PK, group_id FK, member_dn VARCHAR, member_type ENUM user/group, discovered_at DATETIME). attribute_mappings (id INTEGER PK, group_id FK, attribute_key VARCHAR, attribute_value VARCHAR, inferred_by ENUM heuristic/manual, status ENUM candidate/approved/rejected, reviewed_by VARCHAR nullable, reviewed_at DATETIME nullable). abac_policies (id INTEGER PK, group_id FK, policy_name VARCHAR, effect ENUM allow/deny, action VARCHAR, resource_pattern VARCHAR). rego_policy_files (id INTEGER PK, group_id FK, file_path VARCHAR, content_hash CHAR 64, generated_at DATETIME, validated_at DATETIME nullable, validation_passed BOOL nullable). migration_reports (id INTEGER PK, run_at DATETIME, groups_total INT, groups_enumerated INT, groups_mapped INT, groups_approved INT, groups_validated INT, approved_mappings_count INT, pending_review_count INT, validation_errors_json TEXT).
target_output.api_design: CLI subcommands: enumerate [--ldap-url] [--base-dn] [--output-dir] [--batch-size=100] [--apply]. map-attributes [--output-dir] [--apply] (skips groups with all-approved mappings). approve-mapping --group-dn {dn} [--reviewer $USER] [--apply] (sets status=approved for all candidate mappings of group). generate [--output-dir] [--apply] (skips groups with status=rego_approved). validate [--output-dir] [--opa-binary=opa] [--test-fixtures-dir]. report [--output-dir] > migration_report.json. Global flags: --dry-run (default true, overridden by --apply), --log-level, --db-path.
target_output.security_and_compliance: ISO 27001 A.9 compliance: LDAP credentials in LDAP_BIND_DN and LDAP_BIND_PASSWORD env vars only; never persisted. Custom zap core redacts any log field value matching the LDAP_BIND_PASSWORD string. LDAPS enforced by default; --ldap-insecure requires explicit opt-in. Dry-run mode is default; --apply required for any SQLite state writes. Generated Rego files reviewed via PR before OPA deployment; tool never auto-applies. approve-mapping records reviewer identity and timestamp for ISO 27001 change audit trail. migration_report.json retained for access control change management documentation.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Single Go binary ldap-abac-migrator compiled for linux/amd64 via go build. Distributed as tarball to internal tooling server. Prerequisites: OPA 0.60+ binary on PATH (or --opa-binary flag), LDAPS access to corp LDAP, write access to --output-dir. Jenkins CI: go vet, go test -race -coverprofile (80% gate), go build, integration test against OpenLDAP Docker container with 50 seeded groups, validate Rego output against golden files. Jenkins artifact: ldap-abac-migrator-{git-sha}.tar.gz.
target_output.observability: zap JSON logger to stdout and migration.log in --output-dir. Log fields: subcommand, group_dn, status_transition, duration_ms. mpb progress bar for enumerate (groups/total) and validate (files/total). migration_report.json written to --output-dir on report subcommand: includes groups_total, groups_enumerated, groups_mapped, groups_approved, groups_validated, approved_mappings_count, pending_review_count, validation_errors array with group_dn and rego_error fields.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Zero infrastructure cost; runs on existing internal tooling server. At 3000 LDAP groups with 8-worker goroutine pool, enumerate completes in ~15 minutes against a responsive LDAP server. SQLite WAL mode handles concurrent reads during long validate runs. Incremental runs with SQLite checkpointing allow processing in daily batches of 200-300 groups.
target_output.phased_implementation: Week 1: enumerate and SQLite state store, LDAPS connection, credential redaction, Jenkins CI with OpenLDAP fixture. Week 2: map-attributes heuristic inference, approve-mapping subcommand, candidate_mappings review workflow. Week 3: generate Rego files from attribute_mappings, validate via OPA subprocess, regression test fixture format. Week 4: report subcommand with migration_report.json, end-to-end pilot run against 100 production LDAP groups.
target_output.development_guidelines: Go: go vet and staticcheck in CI. go test -race for all store and ldap packages. Table-driven tests for Rego generator covering nested group expansion and edge cases. No credentials in test fixtures or golden files. Conventional commits for changelog. Code review required from IAM security lead before merging changes to generator or validator packages.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: heuristic attribute inference produces incorrect mappings for cryptically named LDAP groups (e.g., cn=grp-947-prod). Mitigation: manual attribute schema file --attribute-schema flag supplements heuristics; approve-mapping workflow forces human review before generate. Tradeoff: OPA subprocess over embedded Go OPA library avoids version coupling but requires OPA binary on PATH in every execution environment. Risk: 3000-group migration spans multiple runs; SQLite corruption on interrupted write. Mitigation: WAL mode and transactional batch commits; integrity_check on SQLite open.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: approve-mapping subcommand approach chosen over YAML review file to keep approval state in SQLite alongside group processing state for consistent reporting. OPA subprocess confirmed over opa-go library to avoid import version conflicts with other internal Go tooling. Incremental batch size defaulting to 100 groups balances LDAP server load and checkpoint granularity.

metadata.notes: Round 4 revision resolving approve-mapping workflow ISS-001 for LDAP-to-ABAC migration CLI tool.
